```text
Now I have a thorough understanding of the codebase. Here is the implementation plan.

---

## Implementation Plan: Fix Google Scholar Citation Badges

### Problem Summary

The current Ruby plugin (`_plugins/google-scholar-citations.rb`) scrapes Google Scholar at Jekyll build time. This fails in GitHub Actions because Google Scholar blocks automated requests (403/CAPTCHA). The site already builds via GitHub Actions (`deploy.yml`), so the plugin does execute, but it cannot reach Google Scholar reliably.

### Architecture Overview

The solution has four parts:

1. A **Python script** that uses the `scholarly` library to fetch citation data and writes it to `_data/scholar_citations.json`
2. A **GitHub Actions workflow** that runs on a schedule (and manually), executes the Python script, commits the JSON data file, which then triggers the existing `deploy.yml` to rebuild the site
3. **Modified `bib.liquid`** that reads citation counts from `site.data.scholar_citations` instead of calling the `{% google_scholar_citations %}` Liquid tag
4. **Modified `publications.md`** that displays a total citation count

---

### Step 1: Create the Python fetching script

**New file: `_scripts/fetch_scholar_citations.py`**

The script should:

- Import `scholarly` and use `scholarly.search_author_by_id("6m8LnLUAAAAJ")` to get the full author profile with all publications in one call (this is more efficient and less likely to be blocked than per-article requests)
- Extract `citedby` (total citations) from the author object
- For each publication in the author's profile, match against known `google_scholar_id` values by checking the `author_pub_id` field (which has the format `userid:articleid`, e.g., `6m8LnLUAAAAJ:9yKSN-GCB0IC`)
- Build a JSON structure like:

```json
{
  "total_citations": 42,
  "last_updated": "2026-03-30T12:00:00Z",
  "articles": {
    "9yKSN-GCB0IC": { "citations": 15, "title": "Sky-Drive..." },
    "2osOgNQ5qMEC": { "citations": 3, "title": "Study of the Tire Wear..." }
  }
}
```

- Write to `_data/scholar_citations.json`
- Include error handling: if `scholarly` fails (network error, blocked), exit with a non-zero code but do NOT overwrite existing data. The workflow should handle this gracefully.
- Use `scholarly.set_timeout(30)` and optionally configure a free proxy via `scholarly.use_proxy(FreeProxies())` as a fallback if direct access fails
- Accept the scholar user ID as a command-line argument or read it from `_config.yml` using PyYAML

Key design decisions:
- Fetch the **entire author profile** in one request rather than per-article. The `scholarly.search_author_by_id(id, fill=True)` call returns all publications with citation counts. This minimizes requests to Google Scholar.
- The script should also parse `papers.bib` to get the list of known `google_scholar_id` values, so it only outputs data for articles that actually exist in the bibliography.

### Step 2: Create the GitHub Actions workflow

**New file: `.github/workflows/update-scholar-citations.yml`**

```yaml
name: Update Scholar Citations

on:
  schedule:
    - cron: "0 4 * * 1"  # Every Monday at 4:00 AM UTC
  workflow_dispatch:  # Allow manual trigger

permissions:
  contents: write

jobs:
  update-citations:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Setup Python
        uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with:
          python-version: "3.13"
          cache: "pip"

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: pip install scholarly pyyaml

      - name: Fetch citation data
        run: python _scripts/fetch_scholar_citations.py
        continue-on-error: true
        id: fetch

      - name: Check for changes
        id: check
        run: |
          if git diff --quiet _data/scholar_citations.json 2>/dev/null; then
            echo "changed=false" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          else
            echo "changed=true" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          fi

      - name: Commit and push
        if: steps.check.outputs.changed == 'true'
        run: |
          git config user.name "github-actions[bot]"
          git config user.email "github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"
          git add _data/scholar_citations.json
          git commit -m "Update Google Scholar citations data"
          git push
```

Key design decisions:
- Weekly schedule (Monday 4 AM UTC) is sufficient for citation counts which change slowly. This minimizes risk of being blocked.
- `workflow_dispatch` allows manual triggering when new papers are added.
- `continue-on-error: true` on the fetch step means a failure does not break the workflow -- it simply keeps the existing data.
- The commit to `main` with a `.json` change will NOT trigger `deploy.yml` because deploy.yml only watches `**.yml`, not `**.json`. **This needs a fix**: either add `"_data/**"` to the deploy.yml paths trigger, or have this workflow directly trigger the deploy workflow via `workflow_dispatch`. The simpler approach is to add `"_data/**"` to the paths list in `deploy.yml`.

### Step 3: Modify `_layouts/bib.liquid` (lines 311-322)

Replace the `{% google_scholar_citations %}` Liquid tag calls with lookups into `site.data.scholar_citations`.

Current code (lines 311-322):
```liquid
{% if site.enable_publication_badges.google_scholar and entry_has_google_scholar_badge %}
  <a href="https://scholar.google.com/citations?view_op=view_citation&hl=en&user={{ site.scholar_userid }}&citation_for_view={{ site.scholar_userid }}:{{ entry.google_scholar_id }}"
     aria-label="Google Scholar link" role="button">
    <img src="https://img.shields.io/badge/scholar-{% google_scholar_citations site.scholar_userid entry.google_scholar_id %}-4285F4?logo=googlescholar&labelColor=beige"
         alt="{% google_scholar_citations site.scholar_userid entry.google_scholar_id %} Google Scholar citations">
  </a>
{% endif %}
```

Replace with:
```liquid
{% if site.enable_publication_badges.google_scholar and entry_has_google_scholar_badge %}
  {% assign gs_data = site.data.scholar_citations.articles[entry.google_scholar_id] %}
  {% if gs_data %}
    {% assign gs_count = gs_data.citations %}
  {% else %}
    {% assign gs_count = "N/A" %}
  {% endif %}
  <a href="https://scholar.google.com/citations?view_op=view_citation&hl=en&user={{ site.scholar_userid }}&citation_for_view={{ site.scholar_userid }}:{{ entry.google_scholar_id }}"
     aria-label="Google Scholar link" role="button">
    <img src="https://img.shields.io/badge/scholar-{{ gs_count }}-4285F4?logo=googlescholar&labelColor=beige"
         alt="{{ gs_count }} Google Scholar citations">
  </a>
{% endif %}
```

This completely decouples the badge rendering from live scraping. The shields.io badge URL is constructed with the pre-fetched count.

### Step 4: Add total citation count to `_pages/publications.md`

Add a total citation count display at the top of the publications page, before the bibliography:

```markdown
---
layout: page
permalink: /publications/
title: Publications
description: <i>To boldly go where no man has gone before.</i>
nav: true
nav_order: 2
---

<!-- _pages/publications.md -->

{% if site.data.scholar_citations %}
<div class="total-citations" style="margin-bottom: 1.5rem;">
  <a href="https://scholar.google.com/citations?user={{ site.scholar_userid }}" target="_blank">
    <img src="https://img.shields.io/badge/Google%20Scholar-{{ site.data.scholar_citations.total_citations }}%20citations-4285F4?logo=googlescholar&labelColor=beige" alt="Total Google Scholar citations">
  </a>
  <small class="text-muted">Last updated: {{ site.data.scholar_citations.last_updated | date: "%B %d, %Y" }}</small>
</div>
{% endif %}

{% include bib_search.liquid %}

<div class="publications">
{% bibliography %}
</div>
```

### Step 5: Handle the transition

1. **Remove or disable the Ruby plugin**: Delete `_plugins/google-scholar-citations.rb` entirely, or rename it to `.rb.bak`. Since `bib.liquid` will no longer call `{% google_scholar_citations %}`, keeping the plugin is harmless but unnecessary. Removing it is cleaner and avoids confusion.

2. **Seed the initial data**: Before the first workflow run, manually run the Python script locally to create `_data/scholar_citations.json` and commit it. This ensures the site works immediately rather than waiting for the first scheduled run.

3. **Update `deploy.yml`**: Add `"_data/**"` to the paths trigger so that when the citation workflow commits updated data, it triggers a site rebuild.

### Step 6: Edge cases and fallbacks

**If `scholarly` gets blocked:**
- The `scholarly` library supports free proxies via `scholarly.use_proxy(FreeProxies())`. The script should try direct access first, then fall back to proxy mode.
- If both fail, the script should exit without modifying the existing JSON file, preserving stale-but-valid data.
- Consider adding a `_data/scholar_citations_manual.json` override mechanism: if this file exists, its values take precedence. This allows manual updates when automated fetching is completely blocked for an extended period.

**If a `google_scholar_id` in `papers.bib` has no match in the fetched profile:**
- The script should log a warning but still include it in the JSON with `"citations": 0` or omit it (so the template shows "N/A").

**shields.io badge URL encoding:**
- Citation counts that are plain integers need no encoding. The "N/A" string is URL-safe. If using formatted numbers like "1.2K", ensure no spaces (the current Ruby plugin already handles this with `.gsub(/\s+/, "")`).

**Rate limiting in the Python script:**
- Add a 5-second delay between retries. Only one request is needed (the author profile fetch), so rate limiting is unlikely.

### File Change Summary

| File | Action | Description |
|------|--------|-------------|
| `_scripts/fetch_scholar_citations.py` | **Create** | Python script to fetch citations via `scholarly` |
| `.github/workflows/update-scholar-citations.yml` | **Create** | Scheduled workflow to run the script and commit data |
| `_data/scholar_citations.json` | **Create** | Data file with citation counts (generated by script) |
| `_layouts/bib.liquid` | **Modify** lines 311-322 | Replace `{% google_scholar_citations %}` with `site.data` lookup |
| `_pages/publications.md` | **Modify** | Add total citation count display |
| `.github/workflows/deploy.yml` | **Modify** | Add `"_data/**"` to paths trigger |
| `_plugins/google-scholar-citations.rb` | **Delete** | No longer needed |

### Implementation Sequence

1. Create `_scripts/fetch_scholar_citations.py` and test locally
2. Run it locally to generate `_data/scholar_citations.json`
3. Modify `_layouts/bib.liquid` to use data file
4. Modify `_pages/publications.md` to show total citations
5. Update `.github/workflows/deploy.yml` paths
6. Create `.github/workflows/update-scholar-citations.yml`
7. Delete `_plugins/google-scholar-citations.rb`
8. Commit all changes together and push

---

### Critical Files for Implementation

- `D:/OneDrive - UW-Madison/My_Github/BillWan-zzzyyy.github.io/_pages/publications.md` - Add total citation count badge display
- `D:/OneDrive - UW-Madison/My_Github/BillWan-zzzyyy.github.io/.github/workflows/deploy.yml` - Add `_data/**` to paths trigger so citation data updates trigger rebuilds
- `D:/OneDrive - UW-Madison/My_Github/BillWan-zzzyyy.github.io/_plugins/google-scholar-citations.rb` - To be deleted; current broken implementation to replace

_**Agent - sidechain (2026-03-31 03:22:33Z)**_

Design an implementation plan for fixing Google Scholar citation badges on an al-folio Jekyll academic website hosted on GitHub Pages.

## Current Problem
The site uses a custom Ruby plugin (`_plugins/google-scholar-citations.rb`) that scrapes Google Scholar pages at build time to get citation counts. This approach fails because:
1. GitHub Pages safe mode doesn't run custom plugins (if using default build)
2. Even with GitHub Actions build, Google Scholar actively blocks scraping (403, CAPTCHA, rate limiting)
3. The badges show "N/A" or fail to render

## Current Implementation
- `_plugins/google-scholar-citations.rb`: Custom Liquid tag that scrapes Google Scholar
- `_layouts/bib.liquid` (lines 311-322): Uses `{% google_scholar_citations site.scholar_userid entry.google_scholar_id %}` to render shields.io badges
- `_bibliography/papers.bib`: Publications with `google_scholar_id` fields (e.g., `9yKSN-GCB0IC`, `2osOgNQ5qMEC`)
- `_config.yml`: `scholar_userid: 6m8LnLUAAAAJ`, `enable_publication_badges.google_scholar: true`
- `_pages/publications.md`: Publications page (needs total citation count added)
- `_includes/selected_papers.liquid`: Selected papers on homepage (uses same bib.liquid template)
- Google Scholar profile: https://scholar.google.com.hk/citations?user=6m8LnLUAAAAJ&hl=en

## Requirements
1. Fix per-paper Google Scholar citation badges (on both publications page and homepage selected papers)
2. Add total citation count badge/display on the publications page
3. Solution must work reliably with GitHub Pages

## Design the Best Approach
The standard approach used by many academic sites is:
1. A GitHub Actions workflow that periodically (e.g., weekly) fetches citation data using Python's `scholarly` library
2. Stores results in a data file (e.g., `_data/scholar_citations.json`) 
3. Jekyll templates read from this data file instead of calling the Ruby plugin
4. Total citation count is also stored and displayed

Please design a detailed implementation plan covering:
- The GitHub Actions workflow file
- The Python script for fetching citations
- Modifications to `bib.liquid` to read from the data file instead of the plugin
- Addition of total citation count to `publications.md`
- How to handle the transition (keeping the plugin as fallback or removing it)
- Any config changes needed

Consider edge cases: what if scholarly gets blocked too? Should we have a manual fallback? What about the shields.io badge format?

    30→    ]
    31→
    32→    # 进程级缓存（本次构建内常驻）
    33→    @@cache = nil
    34→    # 记录上次请求时间，用于控制请求频率
    35→    @@last_request_time = nil
    36→
    37→    def initialize(tag_name, params, tokens)
    38→      super
    39→      splitted = params.split(" ").map(&:strip)
    40→      @scholar_id_var = splitted[0]
    41→      @article_id_var = splitted[1]
    42→    end
    43→
    44→    def render(context)
    45→      # 1) 只在首个调用时读文件缓存
    46→      @@cache ||= self.class.load_cache
    47→
    48→      article_id = context[@article_id_var]
    49→      scholar_id = context[@scholar_id_var]
    50→
    51→      # 2) 判断是否处于“锁定窗口期”
    52→      locked = self.class.locked_window?(@@cache)
    53→
    54→      # 3) 窗口内：优先使用缓存；如果缓存中没有该文章，也尝试抓取（新文章）
    55→      if locked
    56→        cached = self.class.get_cached(@@cache, article_id)
    57→        if cached
    58→          return cached
    59→        else
    60→          # 窗口内但缓存中没有该文章，可能是新添加的，允许抓取一次
    61→          puts "Info: Article #{article_id} not in cache during locked window, attempting fetch..."
    62→        end
    63→      end
    64→
    65→      # 4) 窗口外（需刷新）：尝试抓取；成功后更新缓存与 refreshed_at
    66→      # 控制请求频率：确保每次请求之间至少间隔一定时间
    67→      if @@last_request_time
    68→        time_since_last = Time.now - @@last_request_time
    69→        min_interval = 10.0 # 最小间隔10秒
    70→        if time_since_last < min_interval
    71→          sleep_time = min_interval - time_since_last + rand(2.0..5.0)
    72→          puts "Info: Waiting #{sleep_time.round(1)}s before next request to avoid rate limiting..."
    73→          sleep(sleep_time)
    74→        end
    75→      else
    76→        # 首次请求前的延迟
    77→        sleep(rand(5.0..8.0))
    78→      end
    79→      @@last_request_time = Time.now
    80→
    81→      retries = 0
    82→      begin
    83→        article_url = "https://scholar.google.com/citations?view_op=view_citation&hl=en"\
    84→                      "&user=#{scholar_id}&citation_for_view=#{scholar_id}:#{article_id}"
    85→        
    86→        # 随机选择 User-Agent
    87→        user_agent = USER_AGENTS.sample
    88→        
    89→        # 简化请求头，移除可能暴露爬虫特征的头部
    90→        # 只保留最必要的头部，避免过度模拟
    91→        headers = {
    92→          "User-Agent" => user_agent,
    93→          "Accept" => "text/html,application/xhtml+xml,application/xml;q=0.9,*/*;q=0.8",
    94→          "Accept-Language" => "en-US,en;q=0.9",
    95→          "Accept-Encoding" => "gzip, deflate",
    96→          "Connection" => "keep-alive",
    97→          "Referer" => "https://scholar.google.com/",
    98→          "DNT" => "1"
    99→        }
   100→
   101→        # 使用超时设置打开URL
   102→        # URI.open 的第二个参数直接传递 headers 哈希
   103→        # 使用 Timeout 包装以确保超时控制
   104→        html_content = Timeout.timeout(REQUEST_TIMEOUT) do
   105→          URI.open(article_url, headers).read
   106→        end
   107→        doc = Nokogiri::HTML(html_content)
   108→        citation_count_raw = 0
   109→
   110→        # 方法1: 从 meta description 标签提取
   111→        description_meta = doc.at('meta[name="description"]') || doc.at('meta[property="og:description"]')
   112→        if description_meta
   113→          cited_by_text = description_meta["content"] || ""
   114→          if (m = cited_by_text.match(/Cited by (\d+[,\d]*)/i))
   115→            citation_count_raw = m[1].delete(",").to_i
   116→            puts "Info: Found citation count #{citation_count_raw} from meta description for #{article_id}"
   117→          end
   118→        end
   119→
   120→        # 方法2: 如果方法1失败，尝试从页面文本中提取
   121→        if citation_count_raw == 0
   122→          # 查找包含 "Cited by" 的文本节点
   123→          doc.css('*').each do |element|
   124→            text = element.text
   125→            if text =~ /Cited by\s+(\d+[,\d]*)/i
   126→              citation_count_raw = $1.delete(",").to_i
   127→              puts "Info: Found citation count #{citation_count_raw} from page text for #{article_id}"
   128→              break
   129→            end
   130→          end
   131→        end
   132→
   133→        # 方法3: 尝试从 gsc_oci_value 类中提取（Google Scholar 使用的类名）
   134→        if citation_count_raw == 0
   135→          citation_elem = doc.at('.gsc_oci_value') || doc.at('[class*="gsc_oci"]')
   136→          if citation_elem
   137→            text = citation_elem.text.strip
   138→            if text =~ /(\d+[,\d]*)/
   139→              citation_count_raw = $1.delete(",").to_i
   140→              puts "Info: Found citation count #{citation_count_raw} from gsc_oci element for #{article_id}"
   141→            end
   142→          end
   143→        end
   144→
   145→        # 如果仍然为0，记录警告但继续处理（可能是真的0引用）
   146→        if citation_count_raw == 0
   147→          puts "Warning: Could not extract citation count for #{article_id}, defaulting to 0"
   148→        end
   149→
   150→        # 检查是否被重定向到验证页面或错误页面
   151→        page_title = doc.at('title')&.text&.downcase || ""
   152→        if page_title.include?("sorry") || page_title.include?("unusual traffic") || 
   153→           page_title.include?("captcha") || page_title.include?("verify")
   154→          raise "Google Scholar 检测到异常流量，请稍后重试"
   155→        end
   156→
   157→        # 格式化引用数（小于1000的直接显示数字）
   158→        if citation_count_raw < 1000
   159→          formatted = citation_count_raw.to_s
   160→        else
   161→          formatted = Helpers.number_to_human(
   162→            citation_count_raw,
   163→            format: "%n%u",
   164→            precision: 2,
   165→            units: { thousand: "K", million: "M", billion: "B" }
   166→          )
   167→        end
   168→
   169→        # 确保返回值是 URL 安全的（移除空格和特殊字符，用于 shields.io badge）
   170→        formatted = formatted.strip.gsub(/\s+/, "")
   171→
   172→        # 写入条目
   173→        self.class.set_cached(@@cache, article_id, formatted)
   174→
   175→        # **关键：刷新窗口起点** —— 仅在窗口外（需要全局刷新）时更新
   176→        # 如果在窗口内抓取新文章，不更新全局刷新时间，避免重置窗口
   177→        unless locked
   178→          self.class.set_refreshed!(@@cache)
   179→        end
   180→
   181→        # 持久化到文件
   182→        self.class.save_cache(@@cache)
   183→
   184→        return formatted
   185→      rescue OpenURI::HTTPError => e
   186→        # HTTP 错误处理
   187→        status_code = e.io.status[0].to_i rescue nil
   188→        if status_code == 403
   189→          # 403 Forbidden - 被 Google Scholar 拦截
   190→          puts "Warning: HTTP 403 (Forbidden) for #{article_id} - Google Scholar blocked the request"
   191→          if retries < MAX_RETRIES
   192→            retries += 1
   193→            # 403 错误时增加更长的延迟
   194→            delay = RETRY_DELAY * 2 + rand(5..15)
   195→            puts "Retrying (#{retries}/#{MAX_RETRIES}) after #{delay}s with different approach..."
   196→            sleep(delay)
   197→            # 尝试使用不同的 User-Agent
   198→            retry
   199→          else
   200→            puts "Error: HTTP 403 for #{article_id} after #{MAX_RETRIES} retries - Google Scholar is blocking requests"
   201→            cached = self.class.get_cached(@@cache, article_id)
   202→            return cached if cached
   203→            return "N/A"  # 返回 URL 安全的值
   204→          end
   205→        elsif status_code == 429 || status_code == 503
   206→          # 429 Too Many Requests 或 503 Service Unavailable
   207→          if retries < MAX_RETRIES
   208→            retries += 1
   209→            puts "Warning: HTTP #{status_code} for #{article_id}, retrying (#{retries}/#{MAX_RETRIES}) after #{RETRY_DELAY}s..."
   210→            sleep(RETRY_DELAY + rand(0..5)) # 增加随机延迟
   211→            retry
   212→          else
   213→            puts "Error: HTTP #{status_code} for #{article_id} after #{MAX_RETRIES} retries"
   214→            cached = self.class.get_cached(@@cache, article_id)
   215→            return cached if cached
   216→            return "N/A"  # 返回 URL 安全的值
   217→          end
   218→        else
   219→          puts "Error: HTTP #{status_code || 'unknown'} for #{article_id}: #{e.message}"
   220→          cached = self.class.get_cached(@@cache, article_id)
   221→          return cached if cached
   222→          return "N/A"  # 返回 URL 安全的值
   223→        end
   224→      rescue Timeout::Error, Errno::ETIMEDOUT => e
   225→        # 超时错误
   226→        if retries < MAX_RETRIES
   227→          retries += 1
   228→          puts "Warning: Timeout for #{article_id}, retrying (#{retries}/#{MAX_RETRIES}) after #{RETRY_DELAY}s..."
   229→          sleep(RETRY_DELAY + rand(0..5))
   230→          retry
   231→        else
   232→          puts "Error: Timeout for #{article_id} after #{MAX_RETRIES} retries"
   233→          cached = self.class.get_cached(@@cache, article_id)
   234→          return cached if cached
   235→          return "N/A"  # 返回 URL 安全的值
   236→        end
   237→      rescue StandardError => e
   238→        # 其他错误
   239→        puts "Error fetching citation count for #{article_id}: #{e.class} - #{e.message}"
   240→        cached = self.class.get_cached(@@cache, article_id)
   241→        return cached if cached
   242→        return "N/A"  # 返回 URL 安全的值，避免在 badge URL 中出现特殊字符
   243→      end
   244→    end
   245→
   246→    # --- Cache helpers ---
   247→    class << self
   248→      def load_cache
   249→        return fresh_cache unless File.exist?(CACHE_FILE_PATH)
   250→        begin
   251→          data = JSON.parse(File.read(CACHE_FILE_PATH))
   252→          # 兼容旧结构：没有 _meta 视为需立即刷新
   253→          data["_meta"] ||= {}
   254→          # 验证数据结构
   255→          unless data.is_a?(Hash)
   256→            raise JSON::ParserError, "Invalid cache structure"
   257→          end
   258→          data
   259→        rescue JSON::ParserError, TypeError => e
   260→          puts "Warning: Could not parse scholar citations cache file (#{e.message}). A new one will be created."
   261→          # 备份损坏的缓存文件
   262→          backup_path = "#{CACHE_FILE_PATH}.backup.#{Time.now.to_i}"
   263→          begin
   264→            FileUtils.cp(CACHE_FILE_PATH, backup_path) if File.exist?(CACHE_FILE_PATH)
   265→            puts "Backed up corrupted cache to #{backup_path}"
   266→          rescue => backup_error
   267→            puts "Warning: Could not backup cache file: #{backup_error.message}"
   268→          end
   269→          fresh_cache
   270→        end
   271→      end
   272→
   273→      def save_cache(data)
   274→        FileUtils.mkdir_p(File.dirname(CACHE_FILE_PATH))
   275→        File.open(CACHE_FILE_PATH, "w") { |f| f.write(JSON.pretty_generate(data)) }
   276→      end
   277→
   278→      def fresh_cache
   279→        { "_meta" => { "refreshed_at" => nil } }
   280→      end
   281→
   282→      # 是否处于 24 小时锁定窗口
   283→      def locked_window?(cache)
   284→        refreshed_at_str = cache.dig("_meta", "refreshed_at")
   285→        return false if refreshed_at_str.nil? # 从未刷新过 -> 需要刷新
   286→        refreshed_at = Time.parse(refreshed_at_str) rescue nil
   287→        return false unless refreshed_at
   288→        (Time.now - refreshed_at) < WINDOW_SECONDS
   289→      end
   290→
   291→      def set_refreshed!(cache)
   292→        cache["_meta"] ||= {}
   293→        cache["_meta"]["refreshed_at"] = Time.now.iso8601
   294→      end
   295→
   296→      def get_cached(cache, article_id)
   297→        entry = cache[article_id]
   298→        entry && entry["count"]
   299→      end
   300→
   301→      def set_cached(cache, article_id, formatted_count)
   302→        cache[article_id] = {
   303→          "count" => formatted_count,
   304→          # 为兼容历史分析需要，条目里仍保留单条时间戳（非控制刷新逻辑）
   305→          "timestamp" => Time.now.iso8601
   306→        }
   307→      end
   308→    end
   309→  end
   310→end
   311→
   312→Liquid::Template.register_tag('google_scholar_citations', Jekyll::GoogleScholarCitationsTag)
   313→
```

   204→  limit: 7 # leave blank to include all the news in the `_news` folder
   205→
   206→latest_posts:
   207→  enabled: true
   208→  scrollable: true # adds a vertical scroll bar if there are more than 3 new posts items
   209→  limit: 3 # leave blank to include all the blog posts
   210→
   211→# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
   212→# Jekyll settings
   213→# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
   214→
   215→# Markdown and syntax highlight
   216→markdown: kramdown
   217→highlighter: rouge
   218→kramdown:
   219→  input: GFM
   220→  syntax_highlighter_opts:
   221→    css_class: "highlight"
   222→    span:
   223→      line_numbers: false
   224→    block:
   225→      line_numbers: false
   226→      start_line: 1
   227→
   228→# Includes & excludes
   229→include: ["_pages"]
   230→exclude:
   231→  - bin/
   232→  - CONTRIBUTING.md
   233→  - CUSTOMIZE.md
   234→  - Dockerfile
   235→  - docker-compose.yml
   236→  - docker-compose-slim.yml
   237→  - FAQ.md
   238→  - Gemfile
   239→  - Gemfile.lock
   240→  - INSTALL.md
   241→  - LICENSE
   242→  - lighthouse_results/
   243→  - package.json
   244→  - package-lock.json
   245→  - _pages/about_einstein.md
   246→  - purgecss.config.js
   247→  - README.md
   248→  - readme_preview/
   249→  - vendor
   250→keep_files:
   251→  - CNAME
   252→  - .nojekyll
   253→
   254→# Plug-ins
   255→plugins:
   256→  - jekyll-archives
   257→  - jekyll-email-protect
   258→  - jekyll-feed
   259→  - jekyll-get-json
   260→  - jekyll-imagemagick
   261→  - jekyll-jupyter-notebook
   262→  - jekyll-link-attributes
   263→  - jekyll-minifier
   264→  - jekyll-paginate-v2
   265→  - jekyll-regex-replace
   266→  - jekyll/scholar
   267→  - jekyll-sitemap
   268→  - jekyll-tabs
   269→  - jekyll-toc
   270→  - jekyll-twitter-plugin
   271→  - jemoji
   272→
   273→# Sitemap settings
   274→defaults:
   275→  - scope:
   276→      path: "assets"
   277→    values:
   278→      sitemap: false
   279→
   280→sass:
   281→  style: compressed
   282→
   283→# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
   284→# Jekyll Minifier
   285→# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
   286→
   287→jekyll-minifier:
   288→  exclude: ["robots.txt", "assets/js/search/*.js", "assets/libs/**/*"]
   289→  uglifier_args:
   290→    harmony: true
   291→
   292→# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
   293→# Jekyll Archives
   294→# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
   295→
   296→jekyll-archives:
   297→  enabled: [year, tags, categories] # enables year, tag and category archives (remove if you need to disable one of them).
   298→  layouts:
   299→    year: archive-year
   300→    tag: archive-tag
   301→    category: archive-category
   302→  permalinks:
   303→    year: "/blog/:year/"
   304→    tag: "/blog/tag/:name/"
   305→    category: "/blog/category/:name/"
   306→
   307→display_tags: ["formatting", "images", "links", "math", "code", "blockquotes"] # these tags will be displayed on the front page of your blog
   308→display_categories: ["external-services"] # these categories will be displayed on the front page of your blog
   309→
   310→# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
   311→# Jekyll Scholar
   312→# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
   313→
   314→scholar:
   315→  last_name: [Wan]
   316→  first_name: [Zhengyang]
   317→
   318→  style: apa
   319→  locale: en
   320→
   321→  source: /_bibliography/
   322→  bibliography: papers.bib
   323→  bibliography_template: bib
   324→  # Note: if you have latex math in your bibtex, the latex filter
   325→  # preprocessing may conflict with MathJAX if the latter is enabled.
   326→  # See https://github.com/alshedivat/al-folio/issues/357.
   327→  bibtex_filters: [latex, smallcaps, superscript]
   328→
   329→  replace_strings: true
   330→  join_strings: true
   331→
   332→  details_dir: bibliography
   333→  details_link: Details
   334→
   335→  query: "@*"
   336→  group_by: year
   337→  group_order: descending
   338→
   339→# Display different badges withs stats for your publications
   340→# Customize badge behavior in _layouts/bib.liquid
   341→enable_publication_badges:
   342→  altmetric: true # Altmetric badge (Customization options: https://badge-docs.altmetric.com/index.html)
   343→  dimensions: true # Dimensions badge (Customization options: https://badge.dimensions.ai/)
   344→  google_scholar: true # Google Scholar badge (https://scholar.google.com/intl/en/scholar/citations.html)
   345→  inspirehep: true # Inspire HEP badge (https://help.inspirehep.net/knowledge-base/citation-metrics/)
   346→
   347→# Filter out certain bibtex entry keywords used internally from the bib output
   348→filtered_bibtex_keywords:
   349→  [
   350→    abbr,
   351→    abstract,
   352→    additional_info,
   353→    altmetric,
   354→    annotation,
   355→    arxiv,
   356→    award,
   357→    award_name,
   358→    bibtex_show,
   359→    blog,
   360→    code,
   361→    google_scholar_id,
   362→    html,
   363→    inspirehep_id,
   364→    pdf,
   365→    poster,
   366→    preview,
   367→    selected,
   368→    slides,
   369→    supp,
   370→    video,
   371→    website,
   372→  ]
   373→
   374→# Maximum number of authors to be shown for each publication (more authors are visible on click)
   375→max_author_limit: 3 # leave blank to always show all authors
   376→more_authors_animation_delay: 10 # more authors are revealed on click using animation; smaller delay means faster animation
   377→
   378→# Enables publication thumbnails. If disabled, none of the publications will display thumbnails, even if specified in the bib entry.
   379→enable_publication_thumbnails: true
   380→
   381→# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
   382→# Jekyll Link Attributes
   383→# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
   384→
   385→# These are the defaults
   386→external_links:
   387→  enabled: true
   388→  rel: external nofollow noopener
   389→  target: _blank
   390→  exclude:
   391→
   392→# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
   393→# Responsive WebP Images
   394→# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
   395→
   396→# MAKE SURE imagemagick is installed and on your PATH before enabling imagemagick. In a terminal, run:
   397→#   convert -version
   398→imagemagick:
   399→  enabled: true # enables responsive images for your site (recommended, see https://github.com/alshedivat/al-folio/issues/537)
   400→  widths:
   401→    - 480
   402→    - 800
   403→    - 1400
   404→  input_directories:
   405→    - assets/img/
   406→  input_formats:
   407→    - ".jpg"
   408→    - ".jpeg"
   409→    - ".png"
   410→    - ".tiff"
   411→    - ".gif"
   412→  output_formats:
   413→    webp: "-quality 85"
   414→
   415→# Lazy loading images
   416→#   If you enable lazy loading, all images will add the loading="lazy" attribute.
   417→#   This will make your site load faster, but it may not be supported in all browsers.
   418→#   You can also set loading="" to other values for specific images to override the default behavior.
   419→#   Options: "auto", "eager", "lazy"
   420→#   See https://web.dev/browser-level-image-lazy-loading/ for more information.
   421→lazy_loading_images: true # enables lazy loading of images (recommended)
   422→
   423→# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
   424→# Optional Features
   425→# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
   426→
   427→enable_google_analytics: false # enables google analytics
   428→enable_cronitor_analytics: false # enables cronitor RUM analytics
   429→enable_pirsch_analytics: false # enables Pirsch analytics (https://pirsch.io/)
   430→enable_openpanel_analytics: false # enables Openpanel analytics (https://openpanel.dev/)
   431→enable_google_verification: false # enables google site verification
   432→enable_bing_verification: false # enables bing site verification
   433→enable_masonry: true # enables automatic project cards arrangement
   434→enable_math: true # enables math typesetting (uses MathJax)
   435→enable_tooltips: false # enables automatic tooltip links generated for each section titles on pages and posts
   436→enable_darkmode: true # enables switching between light/dark modes
   437→enable_navbar_social: false # enables displaying social links in the navbar on the about page
   438→enable_project_categories: true # enables categorization of projects into multiple categories
   439→enable_medium_zoom: true # enables image zoom feature (as on medium.com)
   440→enable_progressbar: true # enables a horizontal progress bar linked to the vertical scroll position
   441→enable_video_embedding: false # enables video embedding for bibtex entries. If false, the button opens the video link in a new window.
   442→

Now I have a thorough understanding of the codebase. Here is the implementation plan.

---

## Implementation Plan: Fix Google Scholar Citation Badges

### Problem Summary

The current Ruby plugin (`_plugins/google-scholar-citations.rb`) scrapes Google Scholar at Jekyll build time. This fails in GitHub Actions because Google Scholar blocks automated requests (403/CAPTCHA). The site already builds via GitHub Actions (`deploy.yml`), so the plugin does execute, but it cannot reach Google Scholar reliably.

### Architecture Overview

The solution has four parts:

1. A **Python script** that uses the `scholarly` library to fetch citation data and writes it to `_data/scholar_citations.json`
2. A **GitHub Actions workflow** that runs on a schedule (and manually), executes the Python script, commits the JSON data file, which then triggers the existing `deploy.yml` to rebuild the site
3. **Modified `bib.liquid`** that reads citation counts from `site.data.scholar_citations` instead of calling the `{% google_scholar_citations %}` Liquid tag
4. **Modified `publications.md`** that displays a total citation count

---

### Step 1: Create the Python fetching script

**New file: `_scripts/fetch_scholar_citations.py`**

The script should:

- Import `scholarly` and use `scholarly.search_author_by_id("6m8LnLUAAAAJ")` to get the full author profile with all publications in one call (this is more efficient and less likely to be blocked than per-article requests)
- Extract `citedby` (total citations) from the author object
- For each publication in the author's profile, match against known `google_scholar_id` values by checking the `author_pub_id` field (which has the format `userid:articleid`, e.g., `6m8LnLUAAAAJ:9yKSN-GCB0IC`)
- Build a JSON structure like:

```json
{
  "total_citations": 42,
  "last_updated": "2026-03-30T12:00:00Z",
  "articles": {
    "9yKSN-GCB0IC": { "citations": 15, "title": "Sky-Drive..." },
    "2osOgNQ5qMEC": { "citations": 3, "title": "Study of the Tire Wear..." }
  }
}
```

- Write to `_data/scholar_citations.json`
- Include error handling: if `scholarly` fails (network error, blocked), exit with a non-zero code but do NOT overwrite existing data. The workflow should handle this gracefully.
- Use `scholarly.set_timeout(30)` and optionally configure a free proxy via `scholarly.use_proxy(FreeProxies())` as a fallback if direct access fails
- Accept the scholar user ID as a command-line argument or read it from `_config.yml` using PyYAML

Key design decisions:
- Fetch the **entire author profile** in one request rather than per-article. The `scholarly.search_author_by_id(id, fill=True)` call returns all publications with citation counts. This minimizes requests to Google Scholar.
- The script should also parse `papers.bib` to get the list of known `google_scholar_id` values, so it only outputs data for articles that actually exist in the bibliography.

### Step 2: Create the GitHub Actions workflow

**New file: `.github/workflows/update-scholar-citations.yml`**

```yaml
name: Update Scholar Citations

on:
  schedule:
    - cron: "0 4 * * 1"  # Every Monday at 4:00 AM UTC
  workflow_dispatch:  # Allow manual trigger

permissions:
  contents: write

jobs:
  update-citations:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Setup Python
        uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with:
          python-version: "3.13"
          cache: "pip"

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: pip install scholarly pyyaml

      - name: Fetch citation data
        run: python _scripts/fetch_scholar_citations.py
        continue-on-error: true
        id: fetch

      - name: Check for changes
        id: check
        run: |
          if git diff --quiet _data/scholar_citations.json 2>/dev/null; then
            echo "changed=false" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          else
            echo "changed=true" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          fi

      - name: Commit and push
        if: steps.check.outputs.changed == 'true'
        run: |
          git config user.name "github-actions[bot]"
          git config user.email "github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"
          git add _data/scholar_citations.json
          git commit -m "Update Google Scholar citations data"
          git push
```

Key design decisions:
- Weekly schedule (Monday 4 AM UTC) is sufficient for citation counts which change slowly. This minimizes risk of being blocked.
- `workflow_dispatch` allows manual triggering when new papers are added.
- `continue-on-error: true` on the fetch step means a failure does not break the workflow -- it simply keeps the existing data.
- The commit to `main` with a `.json` change will NOT trigger `deploy.yml` because deploy.yml only watches `**.yml`, not `**.json`. **This needs a fix**: either add `"_data/**"` to the deploy.yml paths trigger, or have this workflow directly trigger the deploy workflow via `workflow_dispatch`. The simpler approach is to add `"_data/**"` to the paths list in `deploy.yml`.

### Step 3: Modify `_layouts/bib.liquid` (lines 311-322)

Replace the `{% google_scholar_citations %}` Liquid tag calls with lookups into `site.data.scholar_citations`.

Current code (lines 311-322):
```liquid
{% if site.enable_publication_badges.google_scholar and entry_has_google_scholar_badge %}
  <a href="https://scholar.google.com/citations?view_op=view_citation&hl=en&user={{ site.scholar_userid }}&citation_for_view={{ site.scholar_userid }}:{{ entry.google_scholar_id }}"
     aria-label="Google Scholar link" role="button">
    <img src="https://img.shields.io/badge/scholar-{% google_scholar_citations site.scholar_userid entry.google_scholar_id %}-4285F4?logo=googlescholar&labelColor=beige"
         alt="{% google_scholar_citations site.scholar_userid entry.google_scholar_id %} Google Scholar citations">
  </a>
{% endif %}
```

Replace with:
```liquid
{% if site.enable_publication_badges.google_scholar and entry_has_google_scholar_badge %}
  {% assign gs_data = site.data.scholar_citations.articles[entry.google_scholar_id] %}
  {% if gs_data %}
    {% assign gs_count = gs_data.citations %}
  {% else %}
    {% assign gs_count = "N/A" %}
  {% endif %}
  <a href="https://scholar.google.com/citations?view_op=view_citation&hl=en&user={{ site.scholar_userid }}&citation_for_view={{ site.scholar_userid }}:{{ entry.google_scholar_id }}"
     aria-label="Google Scholar link" role="button">
    <img src="https://img.shields.io/badge/scholar-{{ gs_count }}-4285F4?logo=googlescholar&labelColor=beige"
         alt="{{ gs_count }} Google Scholar citations">
  </a>
{% endif %}
```

This completely decouples the badge rendering from live scraping. The shields.io badge URL is constructed with the pre-fetched count.

### Step 4: Add total citation count to `_pages/publications.md`

Add a total citation count display at the top of the publications page, before the bibliography:

```markdown
---
layout: page
permalink: /publications/
title: Publications
description: <i>To boldly go where no man has gone before.</i>
nav: true
nav_order: 2
---

<!-- _pages/publications.md -->

{% if site.data.scholar_citations %}
<div class="total-citations" style="margin-bottom: 1.5rem;">
  <a href="https://scholar.google.com/citations?user={{ site.scholar_userid }}" target="_blank">
    <img src="https://img.shields.io/badge/Google%20Scholar-{{ site.data.scholar_citations.total_citations }}%20citations-4285F4?logo=googlescholar&labelColor=beige" alt="Total Google Scholar citations">
  </a>
  <small class="text-muted">Last updated: {{ site.data.scholar_citations.last_updated | date: "%B %d, %Y" }}</small>
</div>
{% endif %}

{% include bib_search.liquid %}

<div class="publications">
{% bibliography %}
</div>
```

### Step 5: Handle the transition

1. **Remove or disable the Ruby plugin**: Delete `_plugins/google-scholar-citations.rb` entirely, or rename it to `.rb.bak`. Since `bib.liquid` will no longer call `{% google_scholar_citations %}`, keeping the plugin is harmless but unnecessary. Removing it is cleaner and avoids confusion.

2. **Seed the initial data**: Before the first workflow run, manually run the Python script locally to create `_data/scholar_citations.json` and commit it. This ensures the site works immediately rather than waiting for the first scheduled run.

3. **Update `deploy.yml`**: Add `"_data/**"` to the paths trigger so that when the citation workflow commits updated data, it triggers a site rebuild.

### Step 6: Edge cases and fallbacks

**If `scholarly` gets blocked:**
- The `scholarly` library supports free proxies via `scholarly.use_proxy(FreeProxies())`. The script should try direct access first, then fall back to proxy mode.
- If both fail, the script should exit without modifying the existing JSON file, preserving stale-but-valid data.
- Consider adding a `_data/scholar_citations_manual.json` override mechanism: if this file exists, its values take precedence. This allows manual updates when automated fetching is completely blocked for an extended period.

**If a `google_scholar_id` in `papers.bib` has no match in the fetched profile:**
- The script should log a warning but still include it in the JSON with `"citations": 0` or omit it (so the template shows "N/A").

**shields.io badge URL encoding:**
- Citation counts that are plain integers need no encoding. The "N/A" string is URL-safe. If using formatted numbers like "1.2K", ensure no spaces (the current Ruby plugin already handles this with `.gsub(/\s+/, "")`).

**Rate limiting in the Python script:**
- Add a 5-second delay between retries. Only one request is needed (the author profile fetch), so rate limiting is unlikely.

### File Change Summary

| File | Action | Description |
|------|--------|-------------|
| `_scripts/fetch_scholar_citations.py` | **Create** | Python script to fetch citations via `scholarly` |
| `.github/workflows/update-scholar-citations.yml` | **Create** | Scheduled workflow to run the script and commit data |
| `_data/scholar_citations.json` | **Create** | Data file with citation counts (generated by script) |
| `_layouts/bib.liquid` | **Modify** lines 311-322 | Replace `{% google_scholar_citations %}` with `site.data` lookup |
| `_pages/publications.md` | **Modify** | Add total citation count display |
| `.github/workflows/deploy.yml` | **Modify** | Add `"_data/**"` to paths trigger |
| `_plugins/google-scholar-citations.rb` | **Delete** | No longer needed |

### Implementation Sequence

1. Create `_scripts/fetch_scholar_citations.py` and test locally
2. Run it locally to generate `_data/scholar_citations.json`
3. Modify `_layouts/bib.liquid` to use data file
4. Modify `_pages/publications.md` to show total citations
5. Update `.github/workflows/deploy.yml` paths
6. Create `.github/workflows/update-scholar-citations.yml`
7. Delete `_plugins/google-scholar-citations.rb`
8. Commit all changes together and push

---

```md
# Fix Google Scholar Citation Badges

## Context
The site's Google Scholar citation badges have been broken because the custom Ruby plugin (`_plugins/google-scholar-citations.rb`) scrapes Google Scholar at Jekyll build time in GitHub Actions. Google Scholar blocks these requests (403/CAPTCHA), causing badges to show "N/A" or fail. The user wants:
1. Fix per-paper citation badges (publications page + homepage selected papers)
2. Add total citation count badge on the publications page

## Approach: GitHub Actions + Data File

Replace the build-time scraping plugin with a **scheduled GitHub Actions workflow** that fetches citation data via Python's `scholarly` library and stores it in `_data/scholar_citations.json`. Jekyll templates read from this data file.

## Files to Create

### 1. `_scripts/fetch_scholar_citations.py`
Python script that:
- Uses `scholarly.search_author_by_id("6m8LnLUAAAAJ", fill=True)` to fetch the full author profile (one request, all papers + total citations)
- Extracts `citedby` (total) and per-publication citation counts
- Matches publications by `author_pub_id` field (format: `userid:articleid`)
- Writes to `_data/scholar_citations.json`:
  \```json
  {
    "total_citations": 42,
    "last_updated": "2026-03-30",
    "articles": {
      "9yKSN-GCB0IC": 15,
      "2osOgNQ5qMEC": 3
    }
  }
  \```
- Error handling: if fetch fails, try with `scholarly.use_proxy(FreeProxies())` as fallback; if still fails, exit without overwriting existing data
- Reads scholar_userid from `_config.yml`

### 2. `.github/workflows/update-scholar-citations.yml`
Scheduled workflow:
- Runs weekly (Monday 4 AM UTC) + manual dispatch
- Installs Python + `scholarly` + `pyyaml`
- Runs the fetch script
- If `_data/scholar_citations.json` changed, commits and pushes (triggers deploy)
- `continue-on-error: true` on fetch step to preserve existing data on failure

### 3. `_data/scholar_citations.json`
Initial seed file created by running the script locally (or manually populated with current counts) so the site works immediately.

## Files to Modify

### 4. `_layouts/bib.liquid` (lines 311-322)
Replace:
\```liquid
{% google_scholar_citations site.scholar_userid entry.google_scholar_id %}
\```
With data file lookup:
\```liquid
{% assign gs_count = site.data.scholar_citations.articles[entry.google_scholar_id] | default: 0 %}
\```
Keep the same shields.io badge format, just use `{{ gs_count }}` instead of the Liquid tag.

### 5. `_pages/publications.md`
Add total citation count badge at the top (before `{% include bib_search.liquid %}`):
\```liquid
{% if site.data.scholar_citations %}
<div style="margin-bottom: 1.5rem;">
  <a href="https://scholar.google.com/citations?user={{ site.scholar_userid }}" target="_blank">
    <img src="https://img.shields.io/badge/Google%20Scholar-{{ site.data.scholar_citations.total_citations }}%20citations-4285F4?logo=googlescholar&labelColor=beige"
         alt="Total Google Scholar citations">
  </a>
</div>
{% endif %}
\```

### 6. `.github/workflows/deploy.yml`
Add `"**.json"` or `"_data/**"` to the paths trigger so citation data commits trigger site rebuild.

## Files to Delete

### 7. `_plugins/google-scholar-citations.rb`
No longer needed — templates will read from data file instead.

## Implementation Sequence
1. Create `_scripts/fetch_scholar_citations.py`
2. Create initial `_data/scholar_citations.json` (seed with known data or run script locally)
3. Modify `_layouts/bib.liquid` — replace plugin tag with data lookup
4. Modify `_pages/publications.md` — add total citation badge
5. Modify `.github/workflows/deploy.yml` — add data path trigger
6. Create `.github/workflows/update-scholar-citations.yml`
7. Delete `_plugins/google-scholar-citations.rb`

## Verification
- Run `bundle exec jekyll build` locally to verify the site builds without errors
- Check that per-paper badges render correctly with citation counts from the JSON data
- Check that total citation badge appears on the publications page
- Verify the GitHub Actions workflow config is valid

```

## Approved Plan:
# Fix Google Scholar Citation Badges

## Context
The site's Google Scholar citation badges have been broken because the custom Ruby plugin (`_plugins/google-scholar-citations.rb`) scrapes Google Scholar at Jekyll build time in GitHub Actions. Google Scholar blocks these requests (403/CAPTCHA), causing badges to show "N/A" or fail. The user wants:
1. Fix per-paper citation badges (publications page + homepage selected papers)
2. Add total citation count badge on the publications page

## Approach: GitHub Actions + Data File

Replace the build-time scraping plugin with a **scheduled GitHub Actions workflow** that fetches citation data via Python's `scholarly` library and stores it in `_data/scholar_citations.json`. Jekyll templates read from this data file.

## Files to Create

### 1. `_scripts/fetch_scholar_citations.py`
Python script that:
- Uses `scholarly.search_author_by_id("6m8LnLUAAAAJ", fill=True)` to fetch the full author profile (one request, all papers + total citations)
- Extracts `citedby` (total) and per-publication citation counts
- Matches publications by `author_pub_id` field (format: `userid:articleid`)
- Writes to `_data/scholar_citations.json`:
  ```json
  {
    "total_citations": 42,
    "last_updated": "2026-03-30",
    "articles": {
      "9yKSN-GCB0IC": 15,
      "2osOgNQ5qMEC": 3
    }
  }
  ```
- Error handling: if fetch fails, try with `scholarly.use_proxy(FreeProxies())` as fallback; if still fails, exit without overwriting existing data
- Reads scholar_userid from `_config.yml`

### 2. `.github/workflows/update-scholar-citations.yml`
Scheduled workflow:
- Runs weekly (Monday 4 AM UTC) + manual dispatch
- Installs Python + `scholarly` + `pyyaml`
- Runs the fetch script
- If `_data/scholar_citations.json` changed, commits and pushes (triggers deploy)
- `continue-on-error: true` on fetch step to preserve existing data on failure

### 3. `_data/scholar_citations.json`
Initial seed file — 实现时先在本地运行 Python 脚本自动从 Google Scholar 抓取真实引用数据生成此文件，然后一并提交。JSON 中的数字示例仅为格式说明，实际值由脚本获取。

## Files to Modify

### 4. `_layouts/bib.liquid` (lines 311-322)
Replace:
```liquid
{% google_scholar_citations site.scholar_userid entry.google_scholar_id %}
```
With data file lookup:
```liquid
{% assign gs_count = site.data.scholar_citations.articles[entry.google_scholar_id] | default: 0 %}
```
Keep the same shields.io badge format, just use `{{ gs_count }}` instead of the Liquid tag.

### 5. `_pages/publications.md`
Add total citation count badge at the top (before `{% include bib_search.liquid %}`):
```liquid
{% if site.data.scholar_citations %}
<div style="margin-bottom: 1.5rem;">
  <a href="https://scholar.google.com/citations?user={{ site.scholar_userid }}" target="_blank">
    <img src="https://img.shields.io/badge/Google%20Scholar-{{ site.data.scholar_citations.total_citations }}%20citations-4285F4?logo=googlescholar&labelColor=beige"
         alt="Total Google Scholar citations">
  </a>
</div>
{% endif %}
```

### 6. `.github/workflows/deploy.yml`
Add `"**.json"` or `"_data/**"` to the paths trigger so citation data commits trigger site rebuild.

## Files to Delete

### 7. `_plugins/google-scholar-citations.rb`
No longer needed — templates will read from data file instead.

## Implementation Sequence
1. Create `_scripts/fetch_scholar_citations.py`
2. Create initial `_data/scholar_citations.json` (seed with known data or run script locally)
3. Modify `_layouts/bib.liquid` — replace plugin tag with data lookup
4. Modify `_pages/publications.md` — add total citation badge
5. Modify `.github/workflows/deploy.yml` — add data path trigger
6. Create `.github/workflows/update-scholar-citations.yml`
7. Delete `_plugins/google-scholar-citations.rb`

## Verification
- Run `bundle exec jekyll build` locally to verify the site builds without errors
- Check that per-paper badges render correctly with citation counts from the JSON data
- Check that total citation badge appears on the publications page
- Verify the GitHub Actions workflow config is valid
```

<summary>Tool use: **Write**</summary>

```python
"""
Fetch Google Scholar citation data and save to _data/scholar_citations.json.

Usage: python _scripts/fetch_scholar_citations.py
"""

import json
import os
import sys
from datetime import datetime, timezone

import yaml

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Config
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
SCRIPT_DIR = os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__))
REPO_ROOT = os.path.dirname(SCRIPT_DIR)
CONFIG_PATH = os.path.join(REPO_ROOT, "_config.yml")
OUTPUT_PATH = os.path.join(REPO_ROOT, "_data", "scholar_citations.json")

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helpers
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def load_scholar_userid():
    with open(CONFIG_PATH, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
        config = yaml.safe_load(f)
    uid = config.get("scholar_userid", "").strip()
    if not uid:
        print("ERROR: scholar_userid not found in _config.yml", file=sys.stderr)
        sys.exit(1)
    return uid


def fetch_author(scholar_id: str):
    """Try direct fetch first; fall back to free proxies if blocked."""
    from scholarly import scholarly, ProxyGenerator

    # Attempt 1: direct connection
    try:
        print(f"Fetching Google Scholar profile for user: {scholar_id} (direct)...")
        author = scholarly.search_author_id(scholar_id)
        author = scholarly.fill(author, sections=["basics", "publications"])
        return author
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"Direct fetch failed: {e}", file=sys.stderr)

    # Attempt 2: free proxies
    try:
        print("Retrying with free proxy...", file=sys.stderr)
        pg = ProxyGenerator()
        pg.FreeProxies()
        scholarly.use_proxy(pg)
        author = scholarly.search_author_id(scholar_id)
        author = scholarly.fill(author, sections=["basics", "publications"])
        return author
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"Proxy fetch also failed: {e}", file=sys.stderr)
        return None


def build_output(author: dict, scholar_id: str) -> dict:
    total = author.get("citedby", 0)
    articles = {}

    for pub in author.get("publications", []):
        pub_id = pub.get("author_pub_id", "")
        # pub_id format: "userid:articleid"
        if ":" in pub_id:
            article_id = pub_id.split(":", 1)[1]
            citations = pub.get("num_citations", 0)
            articles[article_id] = citations

    return {
        "total_citations": total,
        "last_updated": datetime.now(timezone.utc).strftime("%Y-%m-%d"),
        "articles": articles,
    }


def load_existing() -> dict:
    if os.path.exists(OUTPUT_PATH):
        try:
            with open(OUTPUT_PATH, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
                return json.load(f)
        except Exception:
            pass
    return {}


def save_output(data: dict):
    os.makedirs(os.path.dirname(OUTPUT_PATH), exist_ok=True)
    with open(OUTPUT_PATH, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
        json.dump(data, f, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False)
        f.write("\n")
    print(f"Saved to {OUTPUT_PATH}")


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Main
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def main():
    scholar_id = load_scholar_userid()
    author = fetch_author(scholar_id)

    if author is None:
        print(
            "ERROR: Could not fetch citation data. Keeping existing data unchanged.",
            file=sys.stderr,
        )
        if not os.path.exists(OUTPUT_PATH):
            # No existing data; write empty skeleton so Jekyll doesn't error
            save_output({
                "total_citations": 0,
                "last_updated": datetime.now(timezone.utc).strftime("%Y-%m-%d"),
                "articles": {},
            })
        sys.exit(1)

    data = build_output(author, scholar_id)
    print(f"Total citations: {data['total_citations']}")
    for aid, cnt in data["articles"].items():
        print(f"  {aid}: {cnt}")

    save_output(data)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

```

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Write"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Write**</summary>

```yaml
name: Update Scholar Citations

on:
  schedule:
    - cron: "0 4 * * 1" # Every Monday at 4:00 AM UTC
  workflow_dispatch: # Allow manual trigger

permissions:
  contents: write

jobs:
  update-citations:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Setup Python
        uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with:
          python-version: "3.13"
          cache: "pip"

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: pip install scholarly pyyaml

      - name: Fetch citation data
        id: fetch
        continue-on-error: true
        run: python _scripts/fetch_scholar_citations.py

      - name: Check for changes
        id: check
        run: |
          if git diff --quiet _data/scholar_citations.json 2>/dev/null; then
            echo "changed=false" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          else
            echo "changed=true" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          fi

      - name: Commit and push
        if: steps.check.outputs.changed == 'true'
        run: |
          git config user.name "github-actions[bot]"
          git config user.email "github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"
          git add _data/scholar_citations.json
          git commit -m "Update Google Scholar citations data"
          git push

```

# Paper Details Extraction

**Title:** "DriveVLM-RL: Neuroscience-Inspired Reinforcement Learning with Vision-Language Models for Safe and Deployable Autonomous Driving"

**Authors:** Zilin Huang, Zihao Sheng, Zhengyang Wan, Yansong Qu, Junwei You, Sicong Jiang, Sikai Chen

**Publication Venue:** arXiv preprint

**Identifier:** arXiv:2603.18315

**Publication Date:** March 18, 2026

**Volume/Issue/Pages:** Not specified

**Publisher:** Not specified

**DOI:** Not available

## Summary

The paper proposes an integrated framework combining vision-language models with reinforcement learning for autonomous vehicle control. The system uses dual processing pathways—one for continuous spatial safety assessment and another for multi-frame semantic risk analysis. A key innovation involves "removing VLM components at deployment, ensuring real-time feasibility." The research demonstrates improvements in collision avoidance and task success within CARLA simulator environments across varied traffic scenarios.

This paper introduces Sky-Drive, a novel distributed multi-agent simulation platform that addresses these limitations through four key innovations: (a) a distributed architecture for synchronized simulation across multiple terminals; (b) a multi-modal human-in-the-loop framework integrating diverse sensors to collect rich behavioral data; (c) a human-AI collaboration mechanism supporting continuous and adaptive knowledge exchange; and (d) a digital twin (DT) framework for constructing high-fidelity virtual replicas of real-world transportation environments. Future extensions will incorporate foundation models for context-aware decision support and hardware-in-the-loop (HIL) testing for real-world validation. This study develops and validates a multi-body dynamics model of VTT along with a finite element model of its tire. The tire-ground contact behavior and boundary conditions are studied from various perspectives to calculate tire wear. The simulation results demonstrate that the internal pressure of the tires, the load of VTT, the pavement conditions, the inclination angle, and slippage of tires all have critical effects on VTT’s tire wear. These findings suggest that the operating conditions of VTT must be carefully managed to reduce tire wear and ensure safe operation. Furthermore, the research results of tire wear mechanism of VTT offers valuable insights for the optimization and maintenance of VTT.},

_**User (2026-03-31 03:52:49Z)**_

摘要是Ensuring safe decision-making in autonomous vehicles remains a fundamental challenge despite rapid advances in end-to-end learning approaches. Traditional reinforcement learning (RL) methods rely on manually engineered rewards or sparse collision signals, which fail to capture the rich contextual understanding required for safe driving and make unsafe exploration unavoidable in real-world settings. Recent vision-language models (VLMs) offer promising semantic understanding capabilities; however, their high inference latency and susceptibility to hallucination hinder direct application to real-time vehicle control. To address these limitations, this paper proposes DriveVLM-RL, a neuroscience-inspired framework that integrates VLMs into RL through a dual-pathway architecture for safe and deployable autonomous driving. The framework decomposes semantic reward learning into a Static Pathway for continuous spatial safety assessment using CLIP-based contrasting language goals, and a Dynamic Pathway for attention-gated multi-frame semantic risk reasoning using a lightweight detector and a large VLM. A hierarchical reward synthesis mechanism fuses semantic signals with vehicle states, while an asynchronous training pipeline decouples expensive VLM inference from environment interaction. All VLM components are used only during offline training and are removed at deployment, ensuring real-time feasibility. Experiments in the CARLA simulator show significant improvements in collision avoidance, task success, and generalization across diverse traffic scenarios, including strong robustness under settings without explicit collision penalties. These results demonstrate that DriveVLM-RL provides a practical paradigm for integrating foundation models into autonomous driving without compromising real-time feasibility.

# Academic Personal Homepage Design Analysis

## Overall Layout & Visual Structure

This is a minimalist academic portfolio built with Jekyll and the al-folio theme, hosted on GitHub Pages. The design follows a clean, professional aesthetic with a navigation bar at the top and organized content sections flowing vertically down the page.

## Navigation System

The header contains:
- Main navigation links: About (current), Publications, Projects, CV
- A submenu dropdown with Publications, Projects, and Blog
- A search function activated via "ctrl k" using ninja-keys interface
- Theme toggle functionality (light/dark/system)

## Profile Section

Located prominently at the top:
- Large name heading "Zhengyang WAN"
- Professional title: "Ph.D. student at The University of Wisconsin-Madison CEE"
- Social media icon buttons (email, Google Scholar, ORCID, GitHub, LinkedIn)
- WeChat QR code accessible via modal popup
- Recent photo taken in Chicago (2025)

## Main Content Areas

**Introduction Section:**
Features a personal greeting ("Hello there!🤺") followed by educational background spanning Tongji University (B.E. 2022, M.S. 2025) and current PhD enrollment. The text emphasizes research focus on autonomous driving, vision-language-action models, and intelligent transportation systems.

**Recent News Section:**
A chronological timeline including five recent milestones, each dated and marked with emojis, spanning from January 2026 back to September 2024.

**Selected Publications Section:**
Three featured papers displayed with:
- Thumbnail preview images
- Title, authors, publication venue, and year
- Abstract text
- Citation counts from Google Scholar
- Links to HTML versions and videos
- BibTeX citations for academic reference

## Styling & Visual Elements

- Clean typography with hierarchical heading sizes
- Generous whitespace and padding between sections
- Emoji usage throughout for visual personality and engagement
- Horizontal divider lines separating major sections
- Badge elements showing citation counts with color-coded styling
- Responsive image components (university logos, publication previews)

## Footer

Contains copyright notice: "© Copyright 2026 Zhengyang WAN"
- Credits to Jekyll, al-folio theme, and GitHub Pages
- Last update timestamp

## Interactive Features

- Progress bar at top tracking scroll position
- Search modal for site navigation
- Theme switching (light/dark modes)
- WeChat modal popup functionality
- Collapsible mobile navigation menu

## Color Scheme

The design appears to support both light and dark modes, with professional academic styling. Badge elements use Google Scholar blue (#4285F4) and beige backgrounds, maintaining visual hierarchy without overwhelming the reader.

151	  repo: # <your-github-user-name>/<your-github-repo-name>
152	  repo_id: # leave empty or specify your repo_id (see https://giscus.app/)
153	  category: Comments # name of the category under which discussions will be created
154	  category_id: # leave empty or specify your category_id (see https://giscus.app/)
155	  mapping: title # identify discussions by post title
156	  strict: 1 # use strict identification mode
157	  reactions_enabled: 1 # enable (1) or disable (0) emoji reactions
158	  input_position: bottom # whether to display input form below (bottom) or above (top) the comments
159	  theme: preferred_color_scheme # name of the color scheme (preferred works well with al-folio light/dark mode)
160	  emit_metadata: 0
161	  lang: en
162	
163	# Disqus comments (DEPRECATED)
164	disqus_shortname: al-folio # put your disqus shortname
165	# https://help.disqus.com/en/articles/1717111-what-s-a-shortname
166	
167	# External sources.
168	# If you have blog posts published on medium.com or other external sources,
169	# you can display them in your blog by adding a link to the RSS feed.
170	#external_sources:
171	#  - name: medium.com
172	 #   rss_url: https://medium.com/@al-folio/feed
173	#  - name: Google Blog
174	  #  posts:
175	   #   - url: https://blog.google/technology/ai/google-gemini-update-flash-ai-assistant-io-2024/
176	    #    published_date: 2024-05-14
177	
178	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
179	# Newsletter
180	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
181	
182	newsletter:
183	  enabled: false
184	  endpoint: # your loops endpoint (e.g., https://app.loops.so/api/newsletter-form/YOUR-ENDPOINT)
185	  # https://loops.so/docs/forms/custom-form
186	
187	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
188	# Collections
189	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
190	
191	collections:
192	  news:
193	    defaults:
194	      layout: post
195	    output: true
196	    permalink: /:collection/:title/
197	  projects:
198	    output: true
199	    permalink: /:collection/:title/
200	
201	announcements:
202	  enabled: true
203	  scrollable: true # adds a vertical scroll bar if there are more than 3 news items
204	  limit: 7 # leave blank to include all the news in the `_news` folder
205	
206	latest_posts:
207	  enabled: true
208	  scrollable: true # adds a vertical scroll bar if there are more than 3 new posts items
209	  limit: 3 # leave blank to include all the blog posts
210	
211	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
212	# Jekyll settings
213	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
214	
215	# Markdown and syntax highlight
216	markdown: kramdown
217	highlighter: rouge
218	kramdown:
219	  input: GFM
220	  syntax_highlighter_opts:
221	    css_class: "highlight"
222	    span:
223	      line_numbers: false
224	    block:
225	      line_numbers: false
226	      start_line: 1
227	
228	# Includes & excludes
229	include: ["_pages"]
230	exclude:
231	  - bin/
232	  - CONTRIBUTING.md
233	  - CUSTOMIZE.md
234	  - Dockerfile
235	  - docker-compose.yml
236	  - docker-compose-slim.yml
237	  - FAQ.md
238	  - Gemfile
239	  - Gemfile.lock
240	  - INSTALL.md
241	  - LICENSE
242	  - lighthouse_results/
243	  - package.json
244	  - package-lock.json
245	  - _pages/about_einstein.md
246	  - purgecss.config.js
247	  - README.md
248	  - readme_preview/
249	  - vendor
250	keep_files:
251	  - CNAME
252	  - .nojekyll
253	
254	# Plug-ins
255	plugins:
256	  - jekyll-archives
257	  - jekyll-email-protect
258	  - jekyll-feed
259	  - jekyll-get-json
260	  - jekyll-imagemagick
261	  - jekyll-jupyter-notebook
262	  - jekyll-link-attributes
263	  - jekyll-minifier
264	  - jekyll-paginate-v2
265	  - jekyll-regex-replace
266	  - jekyll/scholar
267	  - jekyll-sitemap
268	  - jekyll-tabs
269	  - jekyll-toc
270	  - jekyll-twitter-plugin
271	  - jemoji
272	
273	# Sitemap settings
274	defaults:
275	  - scope:
276	      path: "assets"
277	    values:
278	      sitemap: false
279	
280	sass:
281	  style: compressed
282	
283	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
284	# Jekyll Minifier
285	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
286	
287	jekyll-minifier:
288	  exclude: ["robots.txt", "assets/js/search/*.js", "assets/libs/**/*"]
289	  uglifier_args:
290	    harmony: true
291	
292	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
293	# Jekyll Archives
294	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
295	
296	jekyll-archives:
297	  enabled: [year, tags, categories] # enables year, tag and category archives (remove if you need to disable one of them).
298	  layouts:
299	    year: archive-year
300	    tag: archive-tag
301	    category: archive-category
302	  permalinks:
303	    year: "/blog/:year/"
304	    tag: "/blog/tag/:name/"
305	    category: "/blog/category/:name/"
306	
307	display_tags: ["formatting", "images", "links", "math", "code", "blockquotes"] # these tags will be displayed on the front page of your blog
308	display_categories: ["external-services"] # these categories will be displayed on the front page of your blog
309	
310	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
311	# Jekyll Scholar
312	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
313	
314	scholar:
315	  last_name: [Wan]
316	  first_name: [Zhengyang]
317	
318	  style: apa
319	  locale: en
320	
321	  source: /_bibliography/
322	  bibliography: papers.bib
323	  bibliography_template: bib
324	  # Note: if you have latex math in your bibtex, the latex filter
325	  # preprocessing may conflict with MathJAX if the latter is enabled.
326	  # See https://github.com/alshedivat/al-folio/issues/357.
327	  bibtex_filters: [latex, smallcaps, superscript]
328	
329	  replace_strings: true
330	  join_strings: true
331	
332	  details_dir: bibliography
333	  details_link: Details
334	
335	  query: "@*"
336	  group_by: year
337	  group_order: descending
338	
339	# Display different badges withs stats for your publications
340	# Customize badge behavior in _layouts/bib.liquid
341	enable_publication_badges:
342	  altmetric: true # Altmetric badge (Customization options: https://badge-docs.altmetric.com/index.html)
343	  dimensions: true # Dimensions badge (Customization options: https://badge.dimensions.ai/)
344	  google_scholar: true # Google Scholar badge (https://scholar.google.com/intl/en/scholar/citations.html)
345	  github_stars: true # GitHub stars badge for papers with associated repos
346	  inspirehep: true # Inspire HEP badge (https://help.inspirehep.net/knowledge-base/citation-metrics/)
347	
348	# Filter out certain bibtex entry keywords used internally from the bib output
349	filtered_bibtex_keywords:
350	  [
351	    abbr,
352	    abstract,
353	    additional_info,
354	    altmetric,
355	    annotation,
356	    arxiv,
357	    award,
358	    award_name,
359	    bibtex_show,
360	    blog,
361	    code,
362	    github_repo,
363	    google_scholar_id,
364	    html,
365	    inspirehep_id,
366	    pdf,
367	    poster,
368	    preview,
369	    selected,
370	    slides,
371	    supp,
372	    video,
373	    website,
374	  ]
375	
376	# Maximum number of authors to be shown for each publication (more authors are visible on click)
377	max_author_limit: 3 # leave blank to always show all authors
378	more_authors_animation_delay: 10 # more authors are revealed on click using animation; smaller delay means faster animation
379	
380	# Enables publication thumbnails. If disabled, none of the publications will display thumbnails, even if specified in the bib entry.
381	enable_publication_thumbnails: true
382	
383	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
384	# Jekyll Link Attributes
385	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
386	
387	# These are the defaults
388	external_links:
389	  enabled: true
390	  rel: external nofollow noopener
391	  target: _blank
392	  exclude:
393	
394	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
395	# Responsive WebP Images
396	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
397	
398	# MAKE SURE imagemagick is installed and on your PATH before enabling imagemagick. In a terminal, run:
399	#   convert -version
400	imagemagick:
401	  enabled: true # enables responsive images for your site (recommended, see https://github.com/alshedivat/al-folio/issues/537)
402	  widths:
403	    - 480
404	    - 800
405	    - 1400
406	  input_directories:
407	    - assets/img/
408	  input_formats:
409	    - ".jpg"
410	    - ".jpeg"
411	    - ".png"
412	    - ".tiff"
413	    - ".gif"
414	  output_formats:
415	    webp: "-quality 85"
416	
417	# Lazy loading images
418	#   If you enable lazy loading, all images will add the loading="lazy" attribute.
419	#   This will make your site load faster, but it may not be supported in all browsers.
420	#   You can also set loading="" to other values for specific images to override the default behavior.
421	#   Options: "auto", "eager", "lazy"
422	#   See https://web.dev/browser-level-image-lazy-loading/ for more information.
423	lazy_loading_images: true # enables lazy loading of images (recommended)
424	
425	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
426	# Optional Features
427	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
428	
429	enable_google_analytics: false # enables google analytics
430	enable_cronitor_analytics: false # enables cronitor RUM analytics
431	enable_pirsch_analytics: false # enables Pirsch analytics (https://pirsch.io/)
432	enable_openpanel_analytics: false # enables Openpanel analytics (https://openpanel.dev/)
433	enable_google_verification: false # enables google site verification
434	enable_bing_verification: false # enables bing site verification
435	enable_masonry: true # enables automatic project cards arrangement
436	enable_math: true # enables math typesetting (uses MathJax)
437	enable_tooltips: false # enables automatic tooltip links generated for each section titles on pages and posts
438	enable_darkmode: true # enables switching between light/dark modes
439	enable_navbar_social: false # enables displaying social links in the navbar on the about page
440	enable_project_categories: true # enables categorization of projects into multiple categories
441	enable_medium_zoom: true # enables image zoom feature (as on medium.com)
442	enable_progressbar: true # enables a horizontal progress bar linked to the vertical scroll position
443	enable_video_embedding: false # enables video embedding for bibtex entries. If false, the button opens the video link in a new window.
