```text
1	---
2	layout: about
3	title: About
4	permalink: /
5	subtitle: <span class="highlight-text"> Ph.D. student </span>at The University of Wisconsin-Madison CEE
6	
7	
8	profile:
9	  align: right
10	  image: DSCF1344.webp
11	  image_circular: false # crops the image to make it circular
12	  more_info: >
13	    <p style="margin-left: 35px;">Chicago, US, 2025</p>
14	
15	news: true # includes a list of news items
16	selected_papers: true # includes a list of papers marked as "selected={true}"
17	social: true # includes social icons at the bottom of the page
18	---
19	Hello there!🤺
20	
21	I am a first-year **Ph.D. student** in the Department of Civil and Environmental Engineering at the <img src="../assets/img/uwmadison.png" width="14" height="22" style="vertical-align: middle; margin: 0 2px;"/>[University of Wisconsin-Madison](https://www.wisc.edu/) (UW-Madison). I joined <img src="../assets/img/skylab.png" width="33" height="22" style="vertical-align: middle; margin: 0 2px;"/>[Sky-Lab](https://sky-lab-uw.github.io/) at UW-Madison in Fall 2025 under the guidance of Dr.[Sikai (Sky) Chen](https://sky-lab-uw.github.io/people/). On Wisconsin!<img src="../assets/img/bucky.png" width="15" height="22" style="vertical-align: middle; margin: 0 2px;"/>
22	
23	Before joining UW-Madison, I obtained my M.S. in Transportation Engineering at [College of Transportation](https://tjjt.tongji.edu.cn/), <img src="../assets/img/tongji.png" width="22" height="22" style="vertical-align: middle; margin: 0 2px;"/>Tongji University in 2025, and my B.E. in Vehicle Engineering at [Institute of Rail Transit](https://railway.tongji.edu.cn/main.htm), Tongji University in 2022. I am very fortunate and honored to be advised by Prof. [Hechao Zhou](https://tjjt.tongji.edu.cn/info/2943/10933.htm) and Prof. [Jimin Zhang](https://railway.tongji.edu.cn/c5/e6/c4609a50662/page.htm) during my study at Tongji University. 🫶
24	
25	As for now, I am working to accelerate the development of future intelligent transportation technologies. 🧐 Specifically, I focus on:
26	
27	- **Autonomous driving**
28	- **Vision-Language-Action model**
29	- **Intelligent transportation systems**
30	
31	An equally important dimension of my work is to validate autonomous driving related methods through **real‑vehicle testing**: I have contributed to the development of <img src="../assets/img/Sky-Drive.png" width="25" height="25" style="vertical-align: middle; margin: 0 2px;"/>[SkyDrive](https://github.com/BillWan-zzzyyy/Sky-Drive), an open source multi-agent platform for future transportation system and autonomous driving by Sky-Lab.
32	
33	> 📢***Note:***
34	> **This website is still under development. Please check back later for the latest updates.**
35
```

```text
33	          {% assign profile_image_path = page.profile.image | prepend: 'assets/img/' %}
34	          {% if page.profile.image_circular %}
35	            {% assign profile_image_class = 'img-fluid z-depth-1 rounded-circle' %}
36	          {% else %}
37	            {% assign profile_image_class = 'img-fluid z-depth-1
38	      rounded' %}
39	          {% endif %}
40	          {% capture sizes %}(min-width: {{ site.max_width }}) {{ site.max_width | minus: 30 | times: 0.3}}px, (min-width: 576px)
41	      30vw, 95vw"{% endcapture %}
42	          {%
43	            include figure.liquid loading="eager" path=profile_image_path class=profile_image_class sizes=sizes alt=page.profile.image
44	            cache_bust=true
45	          %}
46	        {% endif %}
47	        {% if page.profile.more_info %}
48	          <div class="more-info">{{ page.profile.more_info }}</div>
49	        {% endif %}

62	      </h6>
63	      {% include news.liquid limit=true %}
64	    {% endif %}

76	    {% endif %}

78	
79	    <!-- Divider -->
```

```text
1	/*******************************************************************************
2	 * Styles for the base elements of the theme.
3	 ******************************************************************************/
4	
5	// Typography
6	
7	p,
8	h1,
9	h2,
10	h3,
11	h4,
12	h5,
13	h6,
14	em,
15	div,
16	li,
17	span,
18	strong {
19	  color: var(--global-text-color);
20	}
21	
22	h2 {
23	  font-weight: 600;
24	}
25	
26	h6 {
27	  font-size: 1.5rem;
28	  font-weight: 600;
29	  display: inline-block; /* 让 h2 的宽度贴合内容，下划线长度会以此为基准 */
30	  position: relative;    /* 关键：为伪元素的绝对定位提供一个锚点 */
31	  padding-bottom: 8px;   /* 可选：为下划线留出足够的空间，避免重叠 */
32	}
33	
34	/* 创建并定义下划线本体 */
35	h6::after {
36	  content: '';         /* 伪元素必须有 content 属性 */
37	  position: absolute;
38	  bottom: 0;           /* 定位在 h2 元素的底部 */
39	  left: 0;             /* 从 h2 元素的左侧开始 */
40	
41	  /* --- 自定义部分 --- */
42	  width: 60px;             /* 1. 自定义长度：可以是百分比(如 50%)或固定值(如 80px) */
43	  height: 6px;             /* 2. 自定义粗细/宽度 */
44	  background-color: var(--global-theme-color);/* 3. 自定义颜色 */
45	}
46	
47	hr {
48	  border-top: 1px solid var(--global-divider-color);
49	}
50	
51	table {
52	  td,
53	  th {
54	    font-size: 1rem;
55	    padding: 1px 1rem 1px 0;
56	  }
57	
58	  th {
59	    font-weight: bold;
60	  }
61	}
62	
63	a,
64	table.table a {
65	  color: var(--global-theme-color);
66	
67	  &:hover {
68	    color: var(--global-theme-color);
69	    text-decoration: underline;
70	  }
71	
72	  &:hover:after :not(.nav-item.dropdown) {
73	    width: 100%;
74	  }
75	}
76	
77	.table-dark {
78	  background-color: transparent;
79	  &.table-bordered {
80	    border: 1px solid var(--global-divider-color) !important;
81	  }
82	}
83	
84	blockquote {
85	  background: var(--global-quoto-block-bg);
86	  border-left: 5px solid var(--global-quote-left);
87	  margin: 1.5em 0;
88	  padding: 1em;
89	  font-size: 1.1rem;
90	
91	  p {
92	    margin-bottom: 0;
93	  }
94	
95	  /* Tips, warnings, and dangers blockquotes */
96	  &.block-tip {
97	    border-color: var(--global-tip-block);
98	    background-color: var(--global-tip-block-bg);
99	
100	    em,
101	    li,
102	    p,
103	    strong {
104	      color: var(--global-tip-block-text);
105	    }
106	
107	    h1,
108	    h2,
109	    h3,
110	    h4,
111	    h5,
112	    h6 {
113	      color: var(--global-tip-block-title);
114	    }
115	  }
116	
117	  &.block-warning {
118	    border-color: var(--global-warning-block);
119	    background-color: var(--global-warning-block-bg);
120	
121	    em,
122	    li,
123	    p,
124	    strong {
125	      color: var(--global-warning-block-text);
126	    }
127	
128	    h1,
129	    h2,
130	    h3,
131	    h4,
132	    h5,
133	    h6 {
134	      color: var(--global-warning-block-title);
135	    }
136	  }
137	
138	  &.block-danger {
139	    border-color: var(--global-danger-block);
140	    background-color: var(--global-danger-block-bg);
141	
142	    em,
143	    li,
144	    p,
145	    strong {
146	      color: var(--global-danger-block-text);
147	    }
148	
149	    h1,
150	    h2,
151	    h3,
152	    h4,
153	    h5,
154	    h6 {
155	      color: var(--global-danger-block-title);
156	    }
157	  }
158	}
159	
160	// Checklist
161	
162	ul.task-list {
163	  list-style-type: none; /* Remove bullets from all levels */
164	}
165	
166	ul.task-list > li ul {
167	  /* Nested lists within .task-list */
168	  padding-inline-start: 1.5rem;
169	}
170	
171	ul.task-list input[type="checkbox"] {
172	  margin: 0.275rem 0.5rem 0.5rem -1rem;
173	  vertical-align: middle;
174	}
175	
176	// Math
177	
178	.equation {
179	  margin-bottom: 1rem;
180	  text-align: center;
181	}
182	
183	// Caption
184	
185	.caption {
186	  font-size: 0.875rem;
187	  margin-top: 0.75rem;
188	  margin-bottom: 1.5rem;
189	  text-align: center;
190	}
191	
192	// Card
193	
194	.card {
195	  background-color: var(--global-card-bg-color);
196	
197	  .card-img {
198	    width: 100%;
199	    padding-top: 1.25rem;
200	  }
201	
202	  .card-title {
203	    color: var(--global-text-color);
204	  }
205	
206	  .card-body {
207	    padding: 1.25rem 1.25rem 1.25rem 1.25rem;
208	  }
209	}
210	
211	// Citation
212	
213	.citation,
214	.citation-number {
215	  color: var(--global-theme-color);
216	}
217	
218	// Profile
219	
220	.profile {
221	  width: 100%;
222	
223	  .more-info {
224	    margin-bottom: 5px;
225	    margin-top: 5px;
226	    font-family: monospace;
227	
228	    p {
229	      display: inline-block;
230	      margin: 0;
231	    }
232	  }
233	}
234	
235	.profile.float-right {
236	  margin-left: 1rem;
237	}
238	
239	.profile.float-left {
240	  margin-right: 1rem;
241	}
242	
243	@media (min-width: 576px) {
244	  .profile {
245	    width: 30%;
246	
247	    .address {
248	      p {
249	        display: block;
250	      }
251	    }
252	  }
253	}
254	
255	.post-description {
256	  margin-bottom: 2rem;
257	  font-size: 0.875rem;
258	
259	  a {
260	    color: inherit;
261	
262	    &:hover {
263	      color: var(--global-theme-color);
264	      text-decoration: none;
265	    }
266	  }
267	}
268	
269	// Navbar customization
270	
271	.navbar {
272	  box-shadow: none;
273	  border-bottom: 1px solid var(--global-divider-color);
274	  background-color: var(--global-bg-color);
275	  opacity: 0.95;
276	}
277	
278	.navbar .dropdown-menu {
279	  background-color: var(--global-bg-color);
280	  border: 1px solid var(--global-divider-color);
281	
282	  a:not(.active) {
283	    color: var(--global-text-color);
284	  }
285	
286	  a:hover {
287	    color: var(--global-hover-color);
288	  }
289	
290	  .dropdown-divider {
291	    border-top: 1px solid var(--global-divider-color) !important;
292	  }
293	}
294	
295	.dropdown-item {
296	  color: var(--global-text-color);
297	
298	  &:hover {
299	    color: var(--global-hover-color);
300	    background-color: var(--global-bg-color);
301	  }
302	}
303	
304	.navbar.navbar-light {
305	  a {
306	    &:hover {
307	      text-decoration: none;
308	    }
309	  }
310	
311	  .navbar-brand {
312	    color: var(--global-text-color);
313	  }
314	
315	  .navbar-nav .nav-item .nav-link {
316	    color: var(--global-text-color);
317	
318	    &:hover {
319	      color: var(--global-hover-color);
320	    }
321	  }
322	
323	  .navbar-nav .nav-item.active > .nav-link {
324	    background-color: inherit;
325	    font-weight: bolder;
326	    color: var(--global-theme-color);
327	
328	    &:hover {
329	      color: var(--global-hover-color);
330	    }
331	  }
332	
333	  .navbar-brand.social {
334	    padding-bottom: 0;
335	    padding-top: 0;
336	    font-size: 1.7rem;
337	
338	    a {
339	      i::before {
340	        color: var(--global-text-color);
341	        transition-property: all 0.2s ease-in-out;
342	      }
343	
344	      &:hover {
345	        i::before {
346	          color: var(--global-theme-color);
347	        }
348	      }
349	    }
350	  }
351	}
352	
353	.navbar-toggler {
354	  .icon-bar {
355	    display: block;
356	    width: 22px;
357	    height: 2px;
358	    background-color: var(--global-text-color);
359	    border-radius: 1px;
360	    margin-bottom: 4px;
361	    transition: all 0.2s;
362	  }
363	
364	  .top-bar {
365	    transform: rotate(45deg);
366	    transform-origin: 10% 10%;
367	  }
368	
369	  .middle-bar {
370	    opacity: 0;
371	  }
372	
373	  .bottom-bar {
374	    transform: rotate(-45deg);
375	    transform-origin: 10% 90%;
376	  }
377	}
378	
379	.navbar-toggler.collapsed {
380	  .top-bar {
381	    transform: rotate(0);
382	  }
383	
384	  .middle-bar {
385	    opacity: 1;
386	  }
387	
388	  .bottom-bar {
389	    transform: rotate(0);
390	  }
391	}
392	
393	#light-toggle,
394	#search-toggle {
395	  padding: 0;
396	  border: 0;
397	  background-color: inherit;
398	  color: var(--global-text-color);
399	
400	  &:hover {
401	    color: var(--global-hover-color);
402	  }
403	}
404	
405	// Social (bottom)
406	
407	.social {
408	  text-align: left;
409	
410	  .contact-icons {
411	    font-size: 2rem;
412	    margin-bottom: 5px;
413	
414	    a {
415	      i::before {
416	        color: var(--global-text-color);
417	        transition-property: all 0.2s ease-in-out;
418	      }
419	
420	      &:hover {
421	        i::before {
422	          color: var(--global-hover-color);
423	        }
424	      }
425	    }
426	  }
427	
428	  .contact-note {
429	    font-size: 0.8rem;
430	  }
431	}
432	
433	.wechat-modal {
434	  display: none;
435	  position: fixed;
436	  z-index: 1;
437	  padding-top: 100px;
438	  left: 0;
439	  top: 0;
440	  width: 100%;
441	  height: 100%;
442	  overflow: auto;
443	  background-color: rgb(0, 0, 0);
444	  background-color: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.8);
445	}
446	
447	.wechat-modal > img {
448	  display: block;
449	  position: relative;
450	  top: 50%;
451	  left: 50%;
452	  width: 33%;
453	  max-width: 400px;
454	  transform: translate(-50%, -50%);
455	}
456	
457	@media only screen and (max-width: 400px) {
458	  .wechat-modal > img {
459	    width: 100%;
460	  }
461	}
462	
463	// Footer
464	footer.fixed-bottom {
465	  background-color: var(--global-footer-bg-color);
466	  font-size: 0.75rem;
467	
468	  .container {
469	    color: var(--global-footer-text-color);
470	    padding-top: 9px;
471	    padding-bottom: 8px;
472	    text-align: center;
473	  }
474	
475	  a {
476	    color: var(--global-footer-link-color);
477	
478	    &:hover {
479	      color: var(--global-theme-color);
480	      text-decoration: none;
481	    }
482	  }
483	}
484	
485	footer.sticky-bottom {
486	  border-top: 1px solid var(--global-divider-color);
487	  padding-top: 40px;
488	  padding-bottom: 40px;
489	  font-size: 0.9rem;
490	
491	  .container {
492	    text-align: center;
493	  }
494	}
495	
496	// CV
497	
498	.cv {
499	  margin-bottom: 40px;
500	
501	  .card {
502	    background-color: var(--global-card-bg-color);
503	    border: 1px solid var(--global-divider-color);
504	
505	    .list-group-item {
506	      background-color: inherit;
507	      border-color: var(--global-divider-color);
508	
509	      .badge {
510	        color: var(--global-card-bg-color) !important;
511	        background-color: var(--global-theme-color) !important;
512	      }
513	    }
514	  }
515	}
516	
517	.table-cv-map {
518	  background-color: transparent;
519	  border: none;
520	  color: var(--global-text-color);
521	}
522	
523	.date-column {

525	  transform: translateX(-15px) translateY(-5px);
526	}
527	
528	// Repositories
529	
530	@media (min-width: 768px) {
531	  .repo {
532	    max-width: 50%;
533	  }
534	}
535	
536	// Blog
537	
538	.header-bar {
539	  border-bottom: 1px solid var(--global-divider-color);
540	  text-align: center;
541	  padding-top: 2rem;
542	  padding-bottom: 3rem;
543	
544	  h1 {
545	    color: var(--global-theme-color);
546	    font-size: 5rem;
547	  }
548	}
549	
550	.tag-category-list {
551	  border-bottom: 1px solid var(--global-divider-color);
552	  text-align: center;
553	  padding-top: 1rem;
554	
555	  ul {
556	    justify-content: center;
557	    display: flow-root;
558	
559	    p,
560	    li {
561	      list-style: none;
562	      display: inline-block;
563	      padding: 1rem 0.5rem;
564	      color: var(--global-text-color-light);
565	    }
566	  }
567	}
568	
569	.post-title {
570	  mjx-container[jax="CHTML"][display="true"] {
571	    display: unset;
572	  }
573	}
574	
575	.post-list {
576	  margin: 0;
577	  margin-bottom: 40px;
578	  padding: 0;
579	
580	  li {
581	    border-bottom: 1px solid var(--global-divider-color);
582	    list-style: none;
583	    padding-top: 2rem;
584	    padding-bottom: 2rem;
585	
586	    .post-meta {
587	      color: var(--global-text-color-light);
588	      font-size: 0.875rem;
589	      margin-bottom: 0;
590	    }
591	
592	    .post-tags {
593	      color: var(--global-text-color-light);
594	      font-size: 0.875rem;
595	      padding-top: 0.25rem;
596	      padding-bottom: 0;
597	    }
598	
599	    a {
600	      color: var(--global-text-color);
601	      text-decoration: none;
602	
603	      &:hover {
604	        color: var(--global-theme-color);
605	      }
606	    }
607	
608	    mjx-container[jax="CHTML"][display="true"] {
609	      display: unset;
610	    }
611	  }
612	}
613	
614	.pagination {
615	  .page-item {
616	    .page-link {
617	      color: var(--global-text-color);
618	      padding: 0.75rem 1.15rem;
619	
620	      &:hover {
621	        color: $black-color;
622	      }
623	    }
624	
625	    &.active .page-link {
626	      color: $white-color;
627	      background-color: var(--global-theme-color);
628	
629	      &:hover {
630	        background-color: var(--global-theme-color);
631	      }
632	    }
633	  }
634	}
635	
636	// Distill
637	
638	.distill {
639	  a:hover {
640	    border-bottom-color: var(--global-theme-color);
641	    text-decoration: none;
642	  }
643	}
644	
645	// Projects
646	
647	.projects {
648	  a {
649	    text-decoration: none;
650	
651	    &:hover {
652	      .card-title {
653	        color: var(--global-theme-color);
654	      }
655	    }
656	  }
657	
658	  .card {
659	    img {
660	      width: 100%;
661	    }
662	  }
663	
664	  .col {
665	    padding-bottom: 1rem;
666	  }
667	
668	  .grid-sizer,
669	  .grid-item {
670	    width: 250px;
671	    margin-bottom: 10px;
672	  }
673	
674	  h2.category {
675	    color: var(--global-divider-color);
676	    border-bottom: 1px solid var(--global-divider-color);
677	    padding-top: 0.5rem;
678	    margin-top: 2rem;
679	    margin-bottom: 1rem;
680	    text-align: right;
681	  }
682	}
683	
684	// Publications
685	
686	.publications {
687	  margin-top: 2rem;
688	
689	  h1 {
690	    color: var(--global-theme-color);
691	    font-size: 2rem;
692	    text-align: center;
693	    margin-top: 1em;
694	    margin-bottom: 1em;
695	  }
696	
697	  h2 {
698	    margin-bottom: 1rem;
699	
700	    span {
701	      font-size: 1.5rem;
702	    }
703	  }
704	
705	  h2.bibliography {
706	    color: var(--global-divider-color);
707	    border-top: 1px solid var(--global-divider-color);
708	    padding-top: 1rem;
709	    margin-top: 2rem;
710	    text-align: right;
711	  }
712	
713	  ol.bibliography {
714	    list-style: none;
715	    padding: 0;
716	    margin-top: 0;
717	
718	    li {
719	      margin-bottom: 1rem;
720	
721	      .preview {
722	      }
723	
724	      .abbr {
725	        margin-bottom: 0.5rem;
726	
727	        abbr {
728	          display: inline-block;
729	          background-color: var(--global-theme-color);
730	          margin-bottom: 0.5rem;
731	
732	          a {
733	            color: white;
734	
735	            &:hover {
736	              text-decoration: none;
737	            }
738	          }
739	        }
740	
741	        .award {
742	          color: var(--global-theme-color) !important;
743	          border: 1px solid var(--global-theme-color);
744	        }
745	      }
746	
747	      .title {
748	        font-weight: 500;
749	      }
750	
751	      .author {
752	        a {
753	          border-bottom: 1px dashed var(--global-theme-color);
754	
755	          &:hover {
756	            border-bottom-style: solid;
757	            text-decoration: none;
758	          }
759	        }
760	
761	        > em {
762	          font-weight: 400;
763	          border-bottom: 1px solid;
764	          font-style: normal;
765	        }
766	
767	        > span.more-authors {
768	          color: var(--global-text-color-light);
769	          border-bottom: 1px dashed var(--global-text-color-light);
770	          cursor: pointer;
771	
772	          &:hover {
773	            color: var(--global-text-color);
774	            border-bottom: 1px dashed var(--global-text-color);
775	          }
776	        }
777	      }
778	
779	      .links {
780	        a.btn {
781	          color: var(--global-text-color);
782	          border: 1px solid var(--global-text-color);
783	          padding-left: 1rem;
784	          padding-right: 1rem;
785	          padding-top: 0.25rem;
786	          padding-bottom: 0.25rem;
787	          margin-left: 0;
788	
789	          &:hover {
790	            color: var(--global-theme-color);
791	            border-color: var(--global-theme-color);
792	          }
793	        }
794	        a.award.btn {
795	          border-color: var(--global-highlight-color);
796	        }
797	      }
798	
799	      .badges {
800	        padding-bottom: 0.5rem;
801	        span {
802	          display: inline-block;
803	          color: $black-color;
804	          height: 100%;
805	          padding-right: 0.5rem;
806	          vertical-align: middle;
807	
808	          &:hover {
809	            text-decoration: underline;
810	          }
811	        }
812	      }
813	
814	      .hidden {
815	        font-size: 0.875rem;
816	        max-height: 0px;
817	        overflow: hidden;
818	        text-align: justify;
819	        transition-property: 0.15s ease;
820	        -moz-transition: 0.15s ease;
821	        -ms-transition: 0.15s ease;
822	        -o-transition: 0.15s ease;
823	        transition: all 0.15s ease;
824	
825	        p {
826	          line-height: 1.4em;
827	          margin: 10px;
828	        }
829	
830	        pre {
831	          font-size: 1em;
832	          line-height: 1.4em;
833	          padding: 10px;
834	        }
835	      }
836	
837	      .hidden.open {
838	        max-height: 100em;
839	        transition-property: 0.15s ease;
840	        -moz-transition: 0.15s ease;
841	        -ms-transition: 0.15s ease;
842	        -o-transition: 0.15s ease;
843	        transition: all 0.15s ease;
844	      }
845	
846	      div.abstract.hidden {
847	        border: dashed 1px var(--global-bg-color);
848	      }
849	
850	      div.abstract.hidden.open {
851	        border-color: var(--global-text-color);
852	      }
853	    }
854	    div.award.hidden {
855	      border: dashed 1px var(--global-bg-color);
856	    }
857	    div.award.hidden.open {
858	      border-color: var(--global-highlight-color);
859	    }
860	  }
861	}
862	
863	// Rouge Color Customization
864	figure.highlight {
865	  margin: 0 0 1rem;
866	}
867	
868	pre {
869	  color: var(--global-theme-color);
870	  background-color: var(--global-code-bg-color);
871	  border-radius: 6px;
872	  padding: 6px 12px;
873	
874	  pre,
875	  code {
876	    background-color: transparent;
877	    border-radius: 0;
878	    margin-bottom: 0;
879	    padding: 0;
880	  }
881	}
882	
883	code {
884	  color: var(--global-theme-color);
885	  background-color: var(--global-code-bg-color);
886	  border-radius: 3px;
887	  padding: 3px 3px;
888	}
889	
890	// Transitioning Themes
891	html.transition,
892	html.transition *,
893	html.transition *:before,
894	html.transition *:after {
895	  transition: all 750ms !important;
896	  transition-delay: 0 !important;
897	}
898	
899	// Extra Markdown style (post Customization)
900	.post {
901	  .post-meta {
902	    color: var(--global-text-color-light);
903	    font-size: 0.875rem;
904	    margin-bottom: 0;
905	  }
906	
907	  .post-tags {
908	    color: var(--global-text-color-light);
909	    font-size: 0.875rem;
910	    padding-top: 0.25rem;
911	    padding-bottom: 1rem;
912	
913	    a {
914	      color: var(--global-text-color-light);
915	      text-decoration: none;
916	
917	      &:hover {
918	        color: var(--global-theme-color);
919	      }
920	    }
921	  }
922	}
923	
924	progress {
925	  /* Positioning */
926	  position: fixed;
927	  left: 0;
928	  top: 56px;
929	  z-index: 10;
930	
931	  /* Dimensions */
932	  width: 100%;
933	  height: 1px;
934	
935	  /* Reset the appearance */
936	  -webkit-appearance: none;
```

```text
12	<!-- pages/projects.md -->
13	<div class="projects">
14	{% if site.enable_project_categories and page.display_categories %}
15	  <!-- Display categorized projects -->
16	  {% for category in page.display_categories %}
17	  <a id="{{ category }}" href=".#{{ category }}">
18	    <h2 class="category">{{ category }}</h2>
19	  </a>
20	  {% assign categorized_projects = site.projects | where: "category", category %}
21	  {% assign sorted_projects = categorized_projects | sort: "importance" %}
22	  <!-- Generate cards for each project -->
23	  {% if page.horizontal %}
24	  <div class="container">
25	    <div class="row row-cols-1 row-cols-md-2">
26	    {% for project in sorted_projects %}

29	    </div>

33	    {% for project in sorted_projects %}

35	    {% endfor %}
36	  </div>
37	  {% endif %}
38	  {% endfor %}
39	
40	{% else %}
41	
42	<!-- Display projects without categories -->
43	
44	{% assign sorted_projects = site.projects | sort: "importance" %}
45	
46	  <!-- Generate cards for each project -->
47	
48	{% if page.horizontal %}
49	
50	  <div class="container">
```

```text
1	---
2	layout: default
3	permalink: /blog/
4	title: blog
5	nav: false
6	nav_order: 1
7	pagination:
8	  enabled: true
9	  collection: posts
10	  permalink: /page/:num/
11	  per_page: 5
12	  sort_field: date
13	  sort_reverse: true
14	  trail:
15	    before: 1 # The number of links before the current page
16	    after: 3 # The number of links after the current page
17	---
18	
19	<div class="post">
20	
21	{% assign blog_name_size = site.blog_name | size %}
22	{% assign blog_description_size = site.blog_description | size %}
23	
24	{% if blog_name_size > 0 or blog_description_size > 0 %}
25	
26	  <div class="header-bar">
27	    <h1>{{ site.blog_name }}</h1>
28	    <h2>{{ site.blog_description }}</h2>
29	  </div>
30	  {% endif %}
31	
32	{% if site.display_tags and site.display_tags.size > 0 or site.display_categories and site.display_categories.size > 0 %}
33	
34	  <div class="tag-category-list">
35	    <ul class="p-0 m-0">
36	      {% for tag in site.display_tags %}
37	        <li>
38	          <i class="fa-solid fa-hashtag fa-sm"></i> <a href="{{ tag | slugify | prepend: '/blog/tag/' | relative_url }}">{{ tag }}</a>
39	        </li>
40	        {% unless forloop.last %}
41	          <p>&bull;</p>
42	        {% endunless %}
43	      {% endfor %}
44	      {% if site.display_categories.size > 0 and site.display_tags.size > 0 %}
45	        <p>&bull;</p>
46	      {% endif %}
47	      {% for category in site.display_categories %}
48	        <li>
49	          <i class="fa-solid fa-tag fa-sm"></i> <a href="{{ category | slugify | prepend: '/blog/category/' | relative_url }}">{{ category }}</a>
50	        </li>
51	        {% unless forloop.last %}
52	          <p>&bull;</p>
53	        {% endunless %}
54	      {% endfor %}
55	    </ul>
56	  </div>
57	  {% endif %}
58	
59	{% assign featured_posts = site.posts | where: "featured", "true" %}
60	{% if featured_posts.size > 0 %}
61	<br>
62	
63	<div class="container featured-posts">
64	{% assign is_even = featured_posts.size | modulo: 2 %}
65	<div class="row row-cols-{% if featured_posts.size <= 2 or is_even == 0 %}2{% else %}3{% endif %}">
66	{% for post in featured_posts %}
67	<div class="col mb-4">
68	<a href="{{ post.url | relative_url }}">
69	<div class="card hoverable">
70	<div class="row g-0">
71	<div class="col-md-12">
72	<div class="card-body">
73	<div class="float-right">
74	<i class="fa-solid fa-thumbtack fa-xs"></i>
75	</div>
76	<h3 class="card-title text-lowercase">{{ post.title }}</h3>
77	<p class="card-text">{{ post.description }}</p>
78	
79	                    {% if post.external_source == blank %}
80	                      {% assign read_time = post.content | number_of_words | divided_by: 180 | plus: 1 %}
81	                    {% else %}
82	                      {% assign read_time = post.feed_content | strip_html | number_of_words | divided_by: 180 | plus: 1 %}
83	                    {% endif %}
84	                    {% assign year = post.date | date: "%Y" %}
85	
86	                    <p class="post-meta">
87	                      {{ read_time }} min read &nbsp; &middot; &nbsp;
88	                      <a href="{{ year | prepend: '/blog/' | prepend: site.baseurl}}">
89	                        <i class="fa-solid fa-calendar fa-sm"></i> {{ year }} </a>
90	                    </p>
91	                  </div>
92	                </div>
93	              </div>
94	            </div>
95	          </a>
96	        </div>
97	      {% endfor %}
98	      </div>
99	    </div>
100	    <hr>
101	
102	{% endif %}
103	
104	  <ul class="post-list">
105	
106	    {% if page.pagination.enabled %}
107	      {% assign postlist = paginator.posts %}
108	    {% else %}
109	      {% assign postlist = site.posts %}
110	    {% endif %}
111	
112	    {% for post in postlist %}
113	
114	    {% if post.external_source == blank %}
115	      {% assign read_time = post.content | number_of_words | divided_by: 180 | plus: 1 %}
116	    {% else %}
117	      {% assign read_time = post.feed_content | strip_html | number_of_words | divided_by: 180 | plus: 1 %}
118	    {% endif %}
119	    {% assign year = post.date | date: "%Y" %}
120	    {% assign tags = post.tags | join: "" %}
121	    {% assign categories = post.categories | join: "" %}

125	{% if post.thumbnail %}
126	
127	<div class="row">

175	    </p>
176	
177	{% if post.thumbnail %}

179	</div>
180

183	  </div>

185	{% endif %}
```

```text
=== VTT.md ===
---
layout: page
title: Driving Simulation for Virtual Track Train
description: Conduct driving simulations of Virtual Track Train's hybrid control mode.
img: assets/img/drivingsimulator.jpg
importance: 1
category: Tongji University
related_publications: true
---

The virtual Track train (VTT) is a novel urban transportation solution that combines the advantages of trams and buses, operating on rubber wheels. Its core technology lies in a unique human/driverless hybrid driving mode. In the driverless mode, the train performs trajectory tracking by recognizing a virtual rail laid on the road surface, while in special conditions, manual control by a driver is required.

Due to its distinctive vehicle structure and control method, research on control algorithms and driving platforms for VTT is still relatively underdeveloped. Most related studies are focused on traditional automotive domains, which to some extent limit its promotion and application and pose potential safety risks.

This project aims to develop control methods for VTT based on an intelligent driving simulation platform, focusing on the following aspects:
```

```text
1	---

3	
4	@article{huang2026drivevlmrl,
5	  title={DriveVLM-RL: Neuroscience-Inspired Reinforcement Learning with Vision-Language Models for Safe and Deployable Autonomous Driving},
6	  author={Huang, Zilin and Sheng, Zihao and Wan, Zhengyang and Qu, Yansong and You, Junwei and Jiang, Sicong and Chen✉, Sikai},
7	  journal={arXiv preprint arXiv:2603.18315},
8	  year={2026},
9	  bibtex_show={true},
10	  abbr={arXiv},
11	  google_scholar_id={Y0pCki6q_DkC},
12	  preview={drivevlmrl.webp},
13	  abstract={Ensuring safe decision-making in autonomous vehicles remains a fundamental challenge despite rapid advances in end-to-end learning approaches. Traditional reinforcement learning (RL) methods rely on manually engineered rewards or sparse collision signals, which fail to capture the rich contextual understanding required for safe driving and make unsafe exploration unavoidable in real-world settings. Recent vision-language models (VLMs) offer promising semantic understanding capabilities; however, their high inference latency and susceptibility to hallucination hinder direct application to real-time vehicle control. To address these limitations, this paper proposes DriveVLM-RL, a neuroscience-inspired framework that integrates VLMs into RL through a dual-pathway architecture for safe and deployable autonomous driving. The framework decomposes semantic reward learning into a Static Pathway for continuous spatial safety assessment using CLIP-based contrasting language goals, and a Dynamic Pathway for attention-gated multi-frame semantic risk reasoning using a lightweight detector and a large VLM. A hierarchical reward synthesis mechanism fuses semantic signals with vehicle states, while an asynchronous training pipeline decouples expensive VLM inference from environment interaction. All VLM components are used only during offline training and are removed at deployment, ensuring real-time feasibility. Experiments in the CARLA simulator show significant improvements in collision avoidance, task success, and generalization across diverse traffic scenarios, including strong robustness under settings without explicit collision penalties. These results demonstrate that DriveVLM-RL provides a practical paradigm for integrating foundation models into autonomous driving without compromising real-time feasibility.},
14	  annotation={* Equal Contribution<br>✉ Corresponding Author},
15	  selected={true}
16	}
17	
18	
19	@article{huang2025sky,
20	  title={Sky-Drive: A Distributed Multi-Agent Simulation Platform for Socially-Aware and Human-AI Collaborative Future Transportation},
21	  author={Huang*, Zilin and Sheng*, Zihao and Wan*, Zhengyang and Qu, Yansong and Luo, Yuhao and Wang, Boyue and Li, Pei and Chen, Yen-Jung and Chen, Jiancong and Long, Keke and Meng, Jiayi and Leng, Yue and Chen✉, Sikai},
22	  journal={Journal of Intelligent and Connected Vehicles},
23	  year={2025},
24	  bibtex_show={true},  
25	  abbr={JICV},
26	  year={2025},
27	  abstract={Recent advances in autonomous system simulation platforms have significantly enhanced the safe and scalable testing of driving policies. However, existing simulators do not yet fully meet the needs of future transportation research, particularly in modeling socially-aware driving agents and enabling effective human-AI collaboration. This paper introduces Sky-Drive, a novel distributed multi-agent simulation platform that addresses these limitations through four key innovations: (a) a distributed architecture for synchronized simulation across multiple terminals; (b) a multi-modal human-in-the-loop framework integrating diverse sensors to collect rich behavioral data; (c) a human-AI collaboration mechanism supporting continuous and adaptive knowledge exchange; and (d) a digital twin (DT) framework for constructing high-fidelity virtual replicas of real-world transportation environments. Sky-Drive supports diverse applications such as autonomous vehicle (AV)-vulnerable road user (VRU) interaction modeling, human-in-the-loop training, socially-aware reinforcement learning, personalized driving policy, and customized scenario generation. Future extensions will incorporate foundation models for context-aware decision support and hardware-in-the-loop (HIL) testing for real-world validation. By bridging scenario generation, data collection, algorithm training, and hardware integration, Sky-Drive has the potential to become a foundational platform for the next generation of socially-aware and human-centered autonomous transportation researc},
28	  html={https://sky-lab-uw.github.io/Sky-Drive-website/},

41	  abbr={SCI Q3},
42	  title={Study of the Tire Wear of Virtual Track Train},
43	  author={Zhou, Hechao and Wan, Zhengyang and Mei, Mingsu and Zhang✉, Jimin and Hecht, Markus},
44	  abstract={The Virtual Track Train (VTT) represents a novel urban transportation solution that combines elements of both road and rail vehicles. Currently, VTT has gained significant adoption in several cities in China including Yibin, Zhuzhou, Harbin, and Yancheng. Despite its growing usage, the unique structure and complex tire-ground interaction of VTT pose significant challenges in terms of tire wear, which has become a pressing issue. This study develops and validates a multi-body dynamics model of VTT along with a finite element model of its tire. The tire-ground contact behavior and boundary conditions are studied from various perspectives to calculate tire wear. The simulation results demonstrate that the internal pressure of the tires, the load of VTT, the pavement conditions, the inclination angle, and slippage of tires all have critical effects on VTT’s tire wear. These findings suggest that the operating conditions of VTT must be carefully managed to reduce tire wear and ensure safe operation. Furthermore, the research results of tire wear mechanism of VTT offers valuable insights for the optimization and maintenance of VTT.},
45	  journal={Proceedings of the Institution of Mechanical Engineers, Part D: Journal of Automobile Engineering},

68	  abstract={The Virtual track train (VTT) has been put into commercial operation as a new type of urban rail transport in China in recent years. To further understand VTT’s operation performance, it is essential to develop a specific driving simulation platform to simulate VTT’s unique hybrid driving mode. The main contribution of this paper are as follows: First, a dynamics model of VTT was established using SIMAPCK. Then, the control module for simulating the hybrid driving mode of VTT was developed based on the model predictive control and Logitech driving simulation hardware. Furthermore, a driving scene visualization interface was established using Unreal Engine and Blender to simulate the real driving environment. These contents constitute the driving simulation platform for VTT. The simulation results show that the developed platform can deliver accurate and comprehensive operation performance of VTT under different driving modes, which has strong engineering significance.},
```

```text
Based on the search results, here are insights into best practices for PhD student personal website design in academic computer science:

## Key Design Principles

Academic websites vary greatly in design and complexity, but ultimately they all achieve the goal they've set out. However, some best practices emerge:

**Content Organization**: Websites should list your publications (if you have any), contact information, and your basic biographical information (school, major, etc.). A good portfolio should link to things like CV, publications with downloadable PDFs, and sample syllabi, and can also share videos created for courses.

**Visual Design**: A picture tells a thousand words, and an effective website interface should contain interesting images to visually educate visitors. PhD student websites can be among the best examples of sharing who you are through the aesthetic look and feel of your personal website.

**Accessibility and Organization**: Colors and branding should flow through each page, and using headings and drop downs helps organize written content for visitors who like to skim when reading.

## Notable Examples

Computer science doctoral students can showcase academic research, internships, and technical work using one-page layouts with emoji accents adding personality to clean professional structure. An ultra-minimal three-page site with professional headshot and clean navigation lets academic work speak for itself without distractions.

## Platform Options

Generic developers like Squarespace and Wix provide ready-to-use templates, while resources aimed specifically at scientists include ScienceSites, Owlstown, and Academic Web Pages, which provide support in website design and maintenance.
```

```text
Based on the search results, here's a comprehensive overview of al-folio theme and its best examples for academic website customization:

## Overview

The al-folio theme is a highly popular Jekyll theme tailored specifically for academics, offering features such as a publications showcase, collections for organizing content, support for math and code, photos integration, GitHub stats integration, theming options, and social media previews.

## Key Features for Customization

The theme comes with default collections for news, projects, books, and teachings, and you can easily create your own collections for apps, stories, courses, or any other creative work.

al-folio supports fast math typesetting through MathJax and code syntax highlighting using GitHub style, and also supports chartjs charts, mermaid diagrams, and TikZ figures.

## Technical Foundation

Content is written in Markdown format, Liquid is used for dynamic template generation, stylesheets are written in SCSS, and Bootstrap 4.6 is used for responsive grid layout and base styling components.

## Real-World Examples

Example implementations include academics at maruan.alshedivat.com and platanios.org, labs like Hay Lab (Caltech) and SAILING Lab, courses like CMU PGM and CMU DeepRL, and conferences & workshops including ICLR Blog Posts (2024) and Neural Compression (ICLR 2021).

## Scaling Capabilities

Because it embraces static-site simplicity, al-folio scales from a single scholar page to lab sites and course hubs, with defaults that encourage good information architecture, accessible design, and fast performance.
```

```text
Here's a comprehensive overview of PhD student homepage design tips and academic website best practices based on the search results:

## Key Strategic Considerations

Start by thinking about your basic communication goals. Consider why you want a web presence given the stage of your career, who will be visiting your website, and what you hope to communicate to these visitors.

You will generally want to make a website by the time you have completed qualifying exams, defended your prospectus, and are considered a PhD candidate.

## Design Best Practices

**Navigation:** Expect that people will spend on average about 30 seconds on your site. Building a simple and intuitive navigation system ensures that visitors find the information they need quickly and efficiently, with visitors able to reach the information they are looking for in less than 3 clicks from the main page.

**Content Presentation:** A picture tells a thousand words. An effective website interface should contain interesting images to visually educate its visitors.

**Writing Style:** When talking about your research, communicate with clear, concise language. Share your expertise with confidence and conviction in what you know, but add a layer of personability.

## Website Structure

The simplest website structure is just 1-page, which tends to work well for graduate student websites and professors who want the easiest website to maintain.

## Platform Options

Generic developers like Squarespace and Wix provide ready-to-use templates and web analytics tools for a monthly fee. There are also resources aimed specifically at scientists, such as ScienceSites, Owlstown, and Academic Web Pages, which provide support in website design, content development, and maintenance for those with little or no site-building experience.
```

```text
# Leonardo Santos PhD Student Website Overview

## Layout & Design
The site uses a clean, minimal academic aesthetic with a responsive navigation bar. It features a professional profile photo and employs the al-folio Jekyll theme, hosted on GitHub Pages. The design prioritizes readability with clear hierarchical sections.

## Navigation Structure
The top navigation includes four main links: About (home), Publications, CV (PDF download), and Book recommendations. These provide quick access to different aspects of the researcher's professional profile.

## Content Sections

**Header Information**
- Name and current affiliation (KTH PhD student)
- Contact details and physical office location
- Profile photograph

**Introduction**
The "About" section contextualizes Santos' research at the intersection of robotics and reinforcement learning, focusing on interpretable robot behavior. It outlines his educational background at UFMG and research experience at CMU through the RISS program.

**News Section**
A chronological timeline displays recent milestones, including graduation, paper acceptances, and program announcements using emoji indicators for visual distinction.

**Selected Publications**
Highlights three peer-reviewed papers with thumbnail previews, publication venues, co-authors, and links to arXiv and project websites.

**Footer**
Includes copyright information, technology credits, and social media links to GitHub, LinkedIn, and Google Scholar.
```

```text
# Max Yang's PhD Student Website

## Layout & Design
The site uses a clean, minimalist academic layout built with Wowchemy, featuring a dark/light/automatic theme toggle. A centered profile section displays Max's photo, title ("PhD Student in Robotics and AI"), and institutional affiliation with the University of Bristol.

## Navigation Structure
A top navigation bar provides access to major sections: Home, Publications, Experience, Projects, and Contact. Social links include email, Google Scholar, GitHub, LinkedIn, and a downloadable CV.

**Publications**: Showcases recent peer-reviewed work with featured thumbnails, PDFs, and external links (2023-2025), focusing on tactile sensing and robotic manipulation.

**Experience**: Timeline format listing current PhD studies, internship at Dexmate AI, prior engineering roles, and undergraduate degree.

**Projects**: Three highlighted projects with descriptions and code repositories, spanning food handling robotics, reinforcement learning algorithms, and optimal control theory.

**Contact**: Email address, location, and LinkedIn messaging option.

## Notable Features
- Responsive image thumbnails for visual interest
- Citation functionality for publications
- Direct links to project websites and video demonstrations
- Academic credentials prominently displayed
```

```text
I've completed the web search for PhD student homepage design tips and academic website best practices. Here's a summary of the key findings:

## Key Design Tips and Best Practices

**Planning & Communication Goals**

The first step in planning your academic web presence is to think about your basic communication goals—given the stage of your career, why do you want a web presence, who will be visiting your website, and what do you hope to communicate to these visitors.

**Navigation & User Experience**

Visitors will spend on average about 30 seconds on your site, so building a simple and intuitive navigation system ensures they find information quickly; visitors should be able to reach the information they are looking for in less than 3 clicks from the main page.

**When to Create Your Website**

You will want to make a website by the time you have completed qualifying exams, defended your prospectus, and are considered a PhD candidate.

**Website Structure**

The simplest website structure is just 1-page, which tends to work well for graduate student websites.

**Content Organization**

You should have documents directly on a page as well as offer download options, which allows people to view your materials more easily.

**Platform Options**

Generic developers like Squarespace and Wix provide ready-to-use templates and web analytics tools, and there are also resources aimed specifically at scientists such as ScienceSites, Owlstown, and Academic Web Pages, which provide support in website design, content development, and maintenance for those with little or no site-building experience.

**Writing Style**

When talking about your research, communicate with clear, concise language and share your expertise with confidence and conviction in what you know, but add a layer of personability.
```

```text
Based on the search results, I found comprehensive information about al-folio theme customization:

## al-folio Theme Overview

The al-folio repository is a widely adopted, open-source Jekyll theme designed for academics and research labs, with opinionated layouts for publications, people, projects, blogs, and CVs, plus features like light/dark mode, math and code support, and social previews.

## Customization Examples

The search results contain several practical customization examples:

**Collections**: The theme comes with two default collections: news and projects, with items from news automatically displayed on the home page and projects displayed on a responsive grid, while you can easily create your own collections by editing the _config.yml file and creating a corresponding folder.

**Theme Colors**: A variety of beautiful theme colors are available, with purple as the default that can be changed by editing the --global-theme-color variable in the _sass/_themes.scss file, and you can add your own colors assigning each a name for ease of use.

**Publications**: Publications are generated from BibTeX using jekyll-scholar, with entries added in _bibliography/papers.bib and the theme rendering citation info, badges, and links.

**Blog Posts**: Blog posts support Distill-style layouts, tabs, galleries, math, mermaid, and charts.

**CV Formats**: The CV can be sourced from a JSON Resume file or fall back to readable YAML.

**Styling**: You can deploy to GitHub Pages or Docker with minimal setup, then customize styling via SCSS.

The search results provided general al-folio information but did not contain specific examples related to autonomous driving, robotics, or PhD applications.
```

```text
## Personal Website
Zilin Huang is a founding member of Sky-Lab at UW-Madison. He is also affiliated with the Center for Connected and Automated Transportation (CCAT) and the Smart Highway Research Center (SHRC). His personal website is available at **https://www.huang-zilin.com/**

## Background
Before joining UW-Madison, Huang worked with Prof. Samuel Labi at Purdue University. Prior to his Ph.D. journey, he founded two technology companies in China.

## Research Focus
His research envisions future transportation systems as a complex human-robot-society ecosystem where humans coexist, interact, and collaborate with diverse autonomous agents that must operate safely and understand human behaviors, preferences, and social norms.

He and his colleagues at Sky-Lab deploy end-to-end driving policies on a lab-developed autonomous van with multimodal perception systems, testing them through real-time streams and extensive testing on public roads and campus testbeds.

## Key Research Areas
His research interests include Human-Centered AI, Human-AI Collaboration, Autonomous Driving, Robotics, and Intelligent Transportation.
```

```text
## Sikai Chen at UW-Madison

Sikai (Sky) Chen is a researcher at the University of Wisconsin-Madison, and he is an Assistant Professor at UW-Madison.

### Sky-Lab Website

The Sky-Lab website is a research group at the University of Wisconsin-Madison. You can access it at: **https://sky-lab-uw.github.io**

The lab's research centers around three major themes: human users, AI, and transportation, with the goal of developing safe, efficient, resilient, and human-centered transportation systems. Their focus is on incorporating human behaviors, interactive autonomy, digital infrastructure, and domain-specific AI.

### Research Areas

Chen's research interests include human-centered AI, autonomous vehicles, reinforcement learning, LLM/VLM (Large Language Models/Vision-Language Models), and roadway safety. The lab has published research on topics such as human-in-the-loop reinforcement learning for safe and efficient autonomous driving.

### Lab Members

The search results also show that the lab has PhD students and researchers working on various autonomous driving projects, including a recently published platform called Sky-Drive, a distributed multi-agent simulation platform that supports diverse applications such as autonomous vehicle–human road user interaction modeling, human-in-the-loop training, and socially-aware reinforcement learning.
```

```text
# Zilin Huang's Academic Homepage: Visual Layout & Design Analysis

## Overall Structure & Navigation

The homepage employs a clean, hierarchical layout with a top navigation bar featuring key sections: About (current), Research, Publications, Projects, Teaching, Talks, Services, Awards, News, and Personal. This horizontal menu provides immediate access to different content areas, establishing clear information architecture for both academic and professional exploration.

## Profile Section Design

The header presents a professional introduction featuring:
- **Visual elements**: A professional headshot photo alongside Chinese characters (姓名 in characters)
- **Key information**: Academic status ("Ph.D. Candidate"), institutional affiliation (UW-Madison), office location, and email
- **Social/Academic Links**: An impressive collection of 10+ connected platforms including Google Scholar, LinkedIn, GitHub, Twitter, ORCID, WeChat, ResearchGate, Semantic Scholar, Hugging Face, and DBLP

This multi-platform approach demonstrates digital presence across diverse academic ecosystems while maintaining professional credibility.

## Content Presentation Strategy

### Research Statement
A prominent, detailed paragraph outlines the researcher's vision for "human-robot-society ecosystems" and autonomous systems. The statement is structured around three key research questions, making the research agenda immediately accessible and intellectually compelling.

### Recent News Section
Features a table-based layout with:
- **Date column** (left-aligned, 12% width)
- **Thumbnail images** (160x100px, left-aligned)
- **News titles** with interactive styling (dashed underline, icon links)
- **Excerpts** and "Read more" links

The styling includes hover effects where news titles darken and borders become solid, indicating interactivity.

### Publications Display

Selected publications showcase a card-based layout featuring:
- **Animated GIFs or teaser images** (research visualizations)
- **Journal abbreviations** (JICV, TR-C, IEEE-TITS)
- **Publication titles** (highlighting "🔥" emoji for notable works)
- **Author lists** with bold formatting for the main author
- **Year indicators**
- **Multiple action links**: PDF, Website, Code repositories with star counts, and Chinese translations

This presentation balances academic rigor with modern design conventions, including fire emojis indicating high-impact or recent contributions.

## Visual Design Elements

**Color Scheme**: The design uses:
- Theme color (appears to be blue-based, used for links and accents)
- Light gray backgrounds (#F5F5F5) for citation containers
- Dark text on white/light backgrounds for readability

**Typography & Spacing**:
- Sections separated by horizontal rules
- H3 headings include decorative underlines (3px solid bar)
- Consistent padding/margin patterns throughout
- Responsive design considerations for mobile devices

## Interactive Features

1. **Hover Effects**: News titles, publication links, and sponsor items respond to mouseover with color changes and transform animations (scale: 1.05)
2. **External Link Indicators**: "🔗" emoji appears after news titles on hover
3. **Dynamic Citation Count**: JavaScript fetches citation data, displaying via Google Scholar integration
4. **Visitor Statistics**: Clustrmaps integration with scaled iframe display and responsive sizing

## Unique Design Choices

- **Multilingual Support**: Chinese character representation and translation links (标题为中文解读)
- **Code Repository Integration**: GitHub links with star count badges normalize research code sharing
- **News Timeline**: Years formatted as section headers with underline styling, creating visual hierarchy
- **Research Focus Presentation**: Bulleted technology interests (Human-in-the-loop Learning, Reinforcement Learning, Foundation Models) provide clear research specialization markers
- **Call-to-Action**: Prominent mention of seeking "tenure-track Assistant Professor positions" with direct contact encouragement

## Content Organization Philosophy

The homepage balances academic authority with approachability. It prioritizes:
- Clear research narrative and vision
- Recent accomplishments and recognition
- Transparent connection to multiple academic ecosystems
- Accessibility through numerous contact/profile options
- Dynamic, contemporary design (GIFs for research visualization)

The design successfully positions the researcher as both academically rigorous and digitally sophisticated, appealing to potential collaborators, employers, and the academic community simultaneously.
```

```text
# Pushkal Mishra's PhD Website: Layout & Design Analysis

## Overall Structure
The site employs a clean, academic-focused design with a linear top-to-bottom organization. Navigation appears minimal, relying on direct links and anchor points rather than traditional menus.

## Key Sections

**Profile Area**
The homepage features a professional introduction, establishing credibility through institutional affiliation and advisor relationships. The site includes a headshot photo and structured biographical data in JSON-LD format for search engine optimization.

**Research Presentation**
Publications are displayed with visual thumbnails—including simulator diagrams and result visualizations—alongside titles and venue information. Each paper includes associated links to project pages, datasets, and BibTeX citations, enabling easy reference management and reproducibility.

**Typography & Visual Hierarchy**
The design uses straightforward heading structures (H1-H3) and institutional logos alongside text descriptions. This creates visual breaks while maintaining academic credibility through official imagery.

## Standout Features

- **Publication Multimedia**: Graphics accompany each paper, illustrating concepts like "A Realistic Radar Simulator for End-to-End Autonomous Driving" rather than relying solely on text
- **Comprehensive Links**: Multiple connection points to Google Scholar, LinkedIn, GitHub, and ORCID enable audience engagement across platforms
- **Timeline Integration**: A "News" section chronologically documents recent achievements, providing transparency about ongoing work
- **Visitor Analytics**: A flag counter displays international traffic, subtly highlighting research reach
- **Acknowledgment of Design Origins**: Footer credit to the original designer and tools used demonstrates transparency
```

```text
# 35 Page Ideas for Academic Personal Websites

Based on the article, here are the suggested pages with relevance to PhD students in engineering/CS:

## Core Pages

**Homepage** - Landing page with brief bio, research description, and navigation menu

**About/Bio** - Extended biography including education, appointments, and professional affiliations

**Awards and Fellowships** - "Finding faculty's past awards and honors on their personal academic website helped me feel excited about opportunities"

**Research** - Overview of research focus and current investigation areas

**Projects/Research Topics** - Individual descriptions of specific research initiatives

**Publications/Writing** - Academic papers, preprints, and written work

## For PhD Students Specifically

**Teaching** - Courses taught or teaching assistant positions held

**Student Resources** - Materials, guides, or support for students in your lab/group

**Speaking Engagements** - Conference presentations and research talks

**Team/Lab Page** - Collaborators, lab members, or research group information

**Join Our Team** - Information for prospective students or postdocs

**Contact** - Information for inquiries and collaboration requests

## Optional Enhancement Pages

**News/Blog** - Updates on research progress and academic activities

**In the Media** - Coverage of research or expertise in external publications

**Community Engagement** - Public-facing research applications or outreach work

**Consulting** - If offering expertise-based services to external parties

**Hobbies** - Personal interests outside academia (optional but humanizing)
```

```md
# Personal Website Improvement Plan

## Context
Based on thorough research of your current website (www.zhengyangwan.com), comparison with exemplary PhD student websites in autonomous driving/CS fields (e.g., Zilin Huang, Pushkal Mishra, contest winners from The Academic Designer), and best practices from Berkeley, Rice, Nature Index, and The Academic Designer guides, I propose the following improvements organized by priority.

---

## Priority 1: Cleanup & Polish (Quick Wins)

### 1.1 Remove Template Remnants
Your site still has default al-folio content that looks unfinished:
- **`_news/announcement_2.md`**: Default demo announcement — delete it
- **`_posts/` (30 files)**: All default demo posts (2015-2024) — delete all of them
- **`_projects/4_project.md` through `9_project.md`**: Template projects — delete them (they're hidden by category filter but clutter the repo)
- **`_pages/profiles.md`**: Still has Einstein placeholder — either customize or keep hidden
- **`_pages/teaching.md`**: Still has placeholder text — either add real content or keep hidden

### 1.2 Remove the "Under Development" Notice
The yellow blockquote warning "This website is still under development" at the bottom of your bio gives a negative first impression. **Visitors spend ~30 seconds on your site** (Berkeley study) — this signals the site isn't ready. Remove it entirely. An academic website is always a living document; no notice is needed.

### 1.3 Fix Outdated Info
- **`assets/json/resume.json`**: `basics.location` still says "Tongji University, Shanghai" — update to UW-Madison, Madison, WI
- **`_config.yml`**: Blog name is still "al-folio" and description is "a simple whitespace theme for academics" — update these even if blog is hidden, since they appear in HTML metadata/SEO

### 1.4 Remove Redundant Dropdown Menu
The "submenus" dropdown at nav_order 8 duplicates Publications and Projects that are already in the main navbar, plus adds Blog which is inactive. **Remove it** — it creates confusion. If you want Blog later, add it as a top-level nav item.
- File: `_pages/dropdown.md` — set `nav: false` or delete

---

## Priority 2: Content Enhancement

### 2.1 Add a Dedicated Research Page
**Problem**: Your research interests are listed as 3 bullet points in the bio, which is too brief. Top PhD student websites (e.g., Zilin Huang) have a dedicated Research page with:
- A **research vision statement** (2-3 paragraphs explaining your overarching research narrative and the problems you want to solve)
- **Research themes** with brief descriptions and representative figures
- How your papers connect to each theme

**Action**: Create `_pages/research.md` with `nav: true, nav_order: 1.5` (between About and Publications), or expand the bio section on the homepage.

### 2.2 Enrich Project Pages
- **VRU project** (`_projects/VRU.md`): Currently redirects to unsplash.com (a stock photo site!) — either add real content or remove this project
- **SkyDrive project** (`_projects/skydrive.md`): Has good content but could add demo videos, GIFs, or more visuals
- Each project should have: problem statement, approach, results/demos, links to code/paper

### 2.3 Add Missing Paper Fields
- **`zhengyang2024vtt`**: Missing `google_scholar_id` — add `google_scholar_id={zYLM7Y9cAGgC}` so the citation badge shows up
- Consider adding `html` or `url` fields for the new DriveVLM-RL paper once it has a project page

### 2.4 Expand Homepage Bio
Your bio is functional but could be stronger. Compared to top PhD sites:
- Add a **one-sentence research vision** at the start (e.g., "My research aims to build safe, human-centered autonomous driving systems that understand and collaborate with humans in complex traffic environments.")
- The "Hello there!🤺" greeting is fine for personality, but consider a more professional opening line followed by the details
- Consider adding a brief mention of key achievements (e.g., total publications, SkyDrive downloads/stars)

---

## Priority 3: Visual Design Improvements

### 3.1 Use Circular Profile Photo
Currently `image_circular: false` — most professional academic sites use a **circular cropped headshot**. Your current photo is casual (captioned "Chicago, US, 2025"). Consider:
- Using a more professional headshot (or crop the current one)
- Setting `image_circular: true` in `_pages/about.md`
- Remove or shorten the caption — "Chicago, US, 2025" adds no academic value

### 3.2 Improve Section Headings
Current `h6` headings with the red underline decoration are visually okay but small. Consider:
- Using `h2` or `h3` for "Recent News" and "Selected Publications" section headers
- Adding icons next to section headings (e.g., 📰 Recent News, 📄 Selected Publications)

### 3.3 ClustrMaps Widget Placement
The visitor counter at the bottom of the homepage (scaled to 70%) feels out of place on the main page. Options:
- **Move it to the footer** or a separate "About" section
- **Replace with a smaller badge-style counter** that's less visually intrusive
- Or simply remove it — most top academic sites don't show visitor counters on the homepage

### 3.4 Publication Preview Images
Your paper previews are good but could be more consistent:
- Ensure all publications have preview images (the new DriveVLM-RL paper has one ✓)
- Use consistent aspect ratios across all preview images
- Consider using animated GIFs for key papers (like Zilin Huang's site does) to make them more eye-catching

---

## Priority 4: Feature Additions

### 4.1 Enable Open Graph & Schema.org Metadata
Currently both are disabled (`serve_og_meta: false`, `serve_schema_org: false`). **Enable them** — this dramatically improves how your site appears when shared on Twitter, LinkedIn, Slack, etc. Your publications and projects will show rich previews with images and descriptions.
- `_config.yml`: Set `serve_og_meta: true` and `serve_schema_org: true`

### 4.2 Add Google Analytics
Currently no analytics are configured. Consider adding Google Analytics or another privacy-friendly analytics tool (e.g., Plausible, Umami) to understand which papers and pages get the most traffic.

### 4.3 Consider Adding a Talks/Presentations Page
If you've given any talks or poster presentations (e.g., "Sky-Drive poster at 2025 Safety Mobility Conference" from your news), a dedicated Talks page with slides/videos builds credibility.

### 4.4 Blog Strategy
You have the blog infrastructure but it's inactive with 30 demo posts. Two options:
- **Option A (Recommended)**: Delete all demo posts, keep blog hidden, and activate it later when you have real content (research summaries, tutorials, conference trip reports)
- **Option B**: Start posting now — even 1-2 research summary posts can drive traffic and demonstrate communication skills

---

## Priority 5: Polish & Refinements

### 5.1 Favicon
Currently using emoji `🧐` as favicon. Consider:
- A custom favicon with your initials "ZW" for a more professional look
- Or keep the emoji if it matches your personal brand

### 5.2 Color Scheme Refinement
Your UW-Madison red (`#c5050c`) as the primary theme color is a strong brand choice. The teal hover color (`#0099cc`) complements it well. This is good — no major changes needed. Minor suggestion:
- The blockquote yellow background (`#fffacd`) with red border is visually strong; consider if you want all blockquotes this prominent, or only specific callouts

### 5.3 CV Page
- Update the PDF filename reference from `CV_ZhengyangWan_2412.pdf` to a more current version if available
- Verify the PDF file exists and is up to date

### 5.4 Footer
The fixed footer with "Powered by Jekyll with al-folio theme" is standard. Consider adding a brief one-liner like "Built with ❤️ at UW-Madison" for personality, but this is very optional.

---

## Summary: Recommended Implementation Order

| Step | Category | Impact | Effort |
|------|----------|--------|--------|
| 1 | Remove "under development" notice | High | 1 min |
| 2 | Delete template remnants (demo posts, projects, news) | High | 10 min |
| 3 | Remove redundant dropdown menu | Medium | 1 min |
| 4 | Fix outdated info (resume.json, config metadata) | Medium | 5 min |
| 5 | Enable Open Graph & Schema.org | Medium | 1 min |
| 6 | Add `google_scholar_id` to VTT paper | Low | 1 min |
| 7 | Circular profile photo | Medium | 1 min |
| 8 | Move/remove ClustrMaps from homepage | Low | 5 min |
| 9 | Fix VRU project (remove unsplash redirect) | Medium | 5 min |
| 10 | Add Research page (optional, needs content) | High | 30+ min |
| 11 | Expand homepage bio with research vision | Medium | 15 min |

## Critical Files
- `_pages/about.md` — Homepage content and profile settings
- `_layouts/about.liquid` — Homepage layout (ClustrMaps, section order)
- `_config.yml` — Site metadata, OG/Schema, blog name
- `assets/json/resume.json` — CV data (location update)
- `_bibliography/papers.bib` — Paper entries (missing google_scholar_id)
- `_pages/dropdown.md` — Redundant dropdown menu
- `_news/announcement_2.md` — Template news to delete
- `_posts/*.md` — 30 demo posts to delete
- `_projects/4-9_project.md` — Template projects to delete
```

```md
# 个人主页改进计划

## 背景
基于对你当前网站 (www.zhengyangwan.com) 的全面分析，以及与同领域优秀博士生主页（如 Zilin Huang、Pushkal Mishra 等）和学术网站设计最佳实践的对比研究，提出以下改进建议，按优先级排序。

---

## 优先级 1：清理与打磨（快速见效）

### 1.1 删除模板残留内容
网站中仍有大量 al-folio 默认内容，显得不够专业：
- **`_news/announcement_2.md`**：默认示例公告 → 删除
- **`_posts/` 下 30 个文件**：全部是默认演示博客文章（2015-2024）→ 全部删除
- **`_projects/4_project.md` 到 `9_project.md`**：模板项目 → 全部删除（虽然因分类过滤被隐藏了，但仍然占据仓库空间）
- **`_pages/profiles.md`**：仍是 Einstein 占位符 → 保持隐藏或自定义
- **`_pages/teaching.md`**：仍是占位文字 → 添加真实内容或保持隐藏

### 1.2 移除"开发中"提示
首页底部的黄色提示框 "This website is still under development" 给访客留下负面第一印象。研究表明**访客平均在你的网站上只停留约 30 秒**——这条提示暗示网站还没准备好。建议直接删除。学术网站本身就是持续更新的，无需特别声明。

### 1.3 修正过时信息
- **`assets/json/resume.json`**：`basics.location` 仍写着"同济大学，上海" → 更新为 UW-Madison, Madison, WI
- **`_config.yml`**：博客名称仍为 "al-folio"，描述仍为 "a simple whitespace theme for academics" → 即使博客隐藏，这些信息也会出现在 HTML metadata/SEO 中，应更新

### 1.4 移除冗余下拉菜单
导航栏中 nav_order 为 8 的 "submenus" 下拉菜单，重复了已在主导航栏中的 Publications 和 Projects，还添加了未启用的 Blog。**建议移除**，避免混淆。
- 文件：`_pages/dropdown.md` → 设置 `nav: false` 或删除

---

## 优先级 2：内容增强

### 2.1 增加专门的研究页面
**现状问题**：你的研究兴趣在首页仅以 3 个 bullet point 列出，过于简略。优秀博士生主页（如 Zilin Huang）通常有专门的 Research 页面，包含：
- **研究愿景陈述**（2-3 段，阐述你的总体研究叙事和想解决的问题）
- **研究方向**（配有简要描述和代表性图片）
- 你的论文如何关联到各个研究方向

**建议操作**：创建 `_pages/research.md`，设置 `nav: true, nav_order: 1.5`（位于 About 和 Publications 之间），或者丰富首页的 bio 部分。

### 2.2 丰富项目页面
- **VRU 项目** (`_projects/VRU.md`)：当前跳转到 unsplash.com（一个图库网站！）→ 添加真实内容或删除此项目
- **SkyDrive 项目** (`_projects/skydrive.md`)：内容不错，可以添加演示视频、GIF 或更多可视化内容
- 每个项目应包含：问题陈述、方法、结果/演示、代码/论文链接

### 2.3 补全论文字段
- **`zhengyang2024vtt`**（VTT 论文）：缺少 `google_scholar_id` → 添加 `google_scholar_id={zYLM7Y9cAGgC}` 以显示引用徽章
- 考虑为新的 DriveVLM-RL 论文添加 `html` 或 `url` 字段（当有项目主页时）

### 2.4 丰富首页简介
你的简介功能上可以，但与顶尖博士生网站相比还有提升空间：
- 在开头添加一句**研究愿景**（如 "My research aims to build safe, human-centered autonomous driving systems that understand and collaborate with humans in complex traffic environments."）
- "Hello there!🤺" 开场体现了个性，但可以考虑在更专业的开场白之后再展开细节
- 考虑简要提及关键成就（如论文总数、SkyDrive 的 GitHub Stars 等）

---

## 优先级 3：视觉设计改进

### 3.1 使用圆形头像
当前设置 `image_circular: false` → 大多数专业学术网站使用**圆形裁剪头像**。当前照片较为休闲（标注 "Chicago, US, 2025"）。建议：
- 使用更专业的头像照片（或裁剪当前照片）
- 在 `_pages/about.md` 中设置 `image_circular: true`
- 移除或缩短图片说明——"Chicago, US, 2025" 对学术展示没有价值

### 3.2 改进分节标题
当前使用 `h6` 标题配红色下划线装饰，视觉上尚可但字号偏小。建议：
- 对 "Recent News" 和 "Selected Publications" 使用 `h2` 或 `h3` 级别标题
- 可在标题旁添加图标（如 📰 Recent News、📄 Selected Publications）

### 3.3 ClustrMaps 访问统计位置
首页底部的访问统计小部件（缩放至 70%）在主页面上显得突兀。建议：
- **移至页脚**或单独的页面
- **替换为更小巧的徽章式计数器**
- 或直接移除——大多数顶尖学术网站不在首页显示访问计数

### 3.4 论文预览图
你的论文预览图不错，但可以更一致：
- 确保所有论文都有预览图（新增的 DriveVLM-RL 已有 ✓）
- 使用一致的宽高比
- 考虑为重要论文使用**动画 GIF**（如 Zilin Huang 的网站那样）使其更吸引眼球

---

## 优先级 4：功能增强

### 4.1 启用 Open Graph 和 Schema.org 元数据
当前两者均禁用（`serve_og_meta: false`、`serve_schema_org: false`）。**强烈建议启用**——这会大幅改善你的网站在 Twitter、LinkedIn、微信、Slack 等平台分享时的展示效果，你的论文和项目会显示富预览（含图片和描述）。
- `_config.yml`：设置 `serve_og_meta: true` 和 `serve_schema_org: true`

### 4.2 添加网站分析
当前没有配置任何分析工具。建议添加 Google Analytics 或隐私友好的替代方案（如 Plausible、Umami），以了解哪些论文和页面获得最多流量。

### 4.3 考虑添加 Talks/报告页面
如果你做过演讲或海报展示（如你 News 中提到的 "Sky-Drive poster at 2025 Safety Mobility Conference"），一个专门的 Talks 页面（配幻灯片/视频）能增强学术可信度。

### 4.4 博客策略
你有博客基础设施但未启用，且保留了 30 篇演示文章。两个选项：
- **方案 A（推荐）**：删除所有演示文章，保持博客隐藏，待有真实内容（研究总结、教程、会议见闻）时再启用
- **方案 B**：现在就开始发布——即使 1-2 篇研究总结文章也能带来流量并展示沟通能力

---

## 优先级 5：细节润色

### 5.1 Favicon 网站图标
当前使用 emoji `🧐` 作为 favicon。建议：
- 制作一个带有你名字缩写 "ZW" 的自定义 favicon，更显专业
- 或者如果 emoji 符合你的个人品牌，保持不变也可以

### 5.2 配色方案
你使用 UW-Madison 红色 (`#c5050c`) 作为主题色是很好的品牌选择，青蓝色悬停效果 (`#0099cc`) 与之互补。整体配色无需大改。小建议：
- 引用框黄色背景 (`#fffacd`) 配红色边框视觉冲击较强，考虑是否所有引用框都需要这么突出

### 5.3 CV 页面
- 更新 PDF 文件名引用，从 `CV_ZhengyangWan_2412.pdf` 更新为最新版本（如有）
- 确认 PDF 文件存在且内容是最新的

### 5.4 页脚
固定页脚中 "Powered by Jekyll with al-folio theme" 是标准做法。可以考虑添加一句个性化文字如 "Built with ❤️ at UW-Madison"，但这非常可选。

---

## 总结：推荐实施顺序

| 步骤 | 类别 | 影响 | 工作量 |
|------|------|------|--------|
| 1 | 移除"开发中"提示 | 高 | 1 分钟 |
| 2 | 删除模板残留（演示文章、项目、公告） | 高 | 10 分钟 |
| 3 | 移除冗余下拉菜单 | 中 | 1 分钟 |
| 4 | 修正过时信息（resume.json、config 元数据） | 中 | 5 分钟 |
| 5 | 启用 Open Graph 和 Schema.org | 中 | 1 分钟 |
| 6 | 为 VTT 论文添加 `google_scholar_id` | 低 | 1 分钟 |
| 7 | 改为圆形头像 | 中 | 1 分钟 |
| 8 | 移动/移除首页 ClustrMaps | 低 | 5 分钟 |
| 9 | 修复 VRU 项目（移除 unsplash 跳转） | 中 | 5 分钟 |
| 10 | 添加 Research 页面（需要撰写内容） | 高 | 30+ 分钟 |
| 11 | 丰富首页简介（添加研究愿景） | 中 | 15 分钟 |

## 涉及的关键文件
- `_pages/about.md` — 首页内容和头像设置
- `_layouts/about.liquid` — 首页布局（ClustrMaps、分节顺序）
- `_config.yml` — 网站元数据、OG/Schema、博客名称
- `assets/json/resume.json` — CV 数据（地址更新）

- `_projects/4-9_project.md` — 模板项目待删除
```
