# Personal Website Improvement Plan

## Context
Based on thorough research of your current website (www.zhengyangwan.com),
comparison with exemplary PhD student websites in autonomous driving/CS fields
(e.g., Zilin Huang, Pushkal Mishra, contest winners from The Academic Designer),
 and best practices from Berkeley, Rice, Nature Index, and The Academic Designer
 guides, I propose the following improvements organized by priority.

---

## Priority 1: Cleanup & Polish (Quick Wins)

### 1.1 Remove Template Remnants
Your site still has default al-folio content that looks unfinished:
- **`_news/announcement_2.md`**: Default demo announcement — delete it
- **`_posts/` (30 files)**: All default demo posts (2015-2024) — delete all of
them
- **`_projects/4_project.md` through `9_project.md`**: Template projects —
delete them (they're hidden by category filter but clutter the repo)
- **`_pages/profiles.md`**: Still has Einstein placeholder — either customize or
 keep hidden
- **`_pages/teaching.md`**: Still has placeholder text — either add real content
 or keep hidden

### 1.2 Remove the "Under Development" Notice
The yellow blockquote warning "This website is still under development" at the
bottom of your bio gives a negative first impression. **Visitors spend ~30
seconds on your site** (Berkeley study) — this signals the site isn't ready.
Remove it entirely. An academic website is always a living document; no notice
is needed.

### 1.3 Fix Outdated Info
- **`assets/json/resume.json`**: `basics.location` still says "Tongji
University, Shanghai" — update to UW-Madison, Madison, WI
- **`_config.yml`**: Blog name is still "al-folio" and description is "a simple
whitespace theme for academics" — update these even if blog is hidden, since
they appear in HTML metadata/SEO

### 1.4 Remove Redundant Dropdown Menu
The "submenus" dropdown at nav_order 8 duplicates Publications and Projects that
 are already in the main navbar, plus adds Blog which is inactive. **Remove it**
 — it creates confusion. If you want Blog later, add it as a top-level nav item.
- File: `_pages/dropdown.md` — set `nav: false` or delete

---

## Priority 2: Content Enhancement

### 2.1 Add a Dedicated Research Page
**Problem**: Your research interests are listed as 3 bullet points in the bio,
which is too brief. Top PhD student websites (e.g., Zilin Huang) have a
dedicated Research page with:
- A **research vision statement** (2-3 paragraphs explaining your overarching
research narrative and the problems you want to solve)
- **Research themes** with brief descriptions and representative figures
- How your papers connect to each theme

**Action**: Create `_pages/research.md` with `nav: true, nav_order: 1.5`
(between About and Publications), or expand the bio section on the homepage.

### 2.2 Enrich Project Pages
- **VRU project** (`_projects/VRU.md`): Currently redirects to unsplash.com (a
stock photo site!) — either add real content or remove this project
- **SkyDrive project** (`_projects/skydrive.md`): Has good content but could add
 demo videos, GIFs, or more visuals
- Each project should have: problem statement, approach, results/demos, links to
 code/paper

### 2.3 Add Missing Paper Fields
- **`zhengyang2024vtt`**: Missing `google_scholar_id` — add
`google_scholar_id={zYLM7Y9cAGgC}` so the citation badge shows up
- Consider adding `html` or `url` fields for the new DriveVLM-RL paper once it
has a project page

### 2.4 Expand Homepage Bio
Your bio is functional but could be stronger. Compared to top PhD sites:
- Add a **one-sentence research vision** at the start (e.g., "My research aims
to build safe, human-centered autonomous driving systems that understand and
collaborate with humans in complex traffic environments.")
- The "Hello there!🤺" greeting is fine for personality, but consider a more
professional opening line followed by the details
- Consider adding a brief mention of key achievements (e.g., total publications,
 SkyDrive downloads/stars)

---

## Priority 3: Visual Design Improvements

### 3.1 Use Circular Profile Photo
Currently `image_circular: false` — most professional academic sites use a
**circular cropped headshot**. Your current photo is casual (captioned "Chicago,
 US, 2025"). Consider:
- Using a more professional headshot (or crop the current one)
- Setting `image_circular: true` in `_pages/about.md`
- Remove or shorten the caption — "Chicago, US, 2025" adds no academic value

### 3.2 Improve Section Headings
Current `h6` headings with the red underline decoration are visually okay but
small. Consider:
- Using `h2` or `h3` for "Recent News" and "Selected Publications" section
headers
- Adding icons next to section headings (e.g., 📰 Recent News, 📄 Selected
Publications)

### 3.3 ClustrMaps Widget Placement
The visitor counter at the bottom of the homepage (scaled to 70%) feels out of
place on the main page. Options:
- **Move it to the footer** or a separate "About" section
- **Replace with a smaller badge-style counter** that's less visually intrusive
- Or simply remove it — most top academic sites don't show visitor counters on
the homepage

### 3.4 Publication Preview Images
Your paper previews are good but could be more consistent:
- Ensure all publications have preview images (the new DriveVLM-RL paper has one
 ✓)
- Use consistent aspect ratios across all preview images
- Consider using animated GIFs for key papers (like Zilin Huang's site does) to
make them more eye-catching

---

## Priority 4: Feature Additions

### 4.1 Enable Open Graph & Schema.org Metadata
Currently both are disabled (`serve_og_meta: false`, `serve_schema_org: false`).
 **Enable them** — this dramatically improves how your site appears when shared
on Twitter, LinkedIn, Slack, etc. Your publications and projects will show rich
previews with images and descriptions.
- `_config.yml`: Set `serve_og_meta: true` and `serve_schema_org: true`

### 4.2 Add Google Analytics
Currently no analytics are configured. Consider adding Google Analytics or
another privacy-friendly analytics tool (e.g., Plausible, Umami) to understand
which papers and pages get the most traffic.

### 4.3 Consider Adding a Talks/Presentations Page
If you've given any talks or poster presentations (e.g., "Sky-Drive poster at
2025 Safety Mobility Conference" from your news), a dedicated Talks page with
slides/videos builds credibility.

### 4.4 Blog Strategy
You have the blog infrastructure but it's inactive with 30 demo posts. Two
options:
- **Option A (Recommended)**: Delete all demo posts, keep blog hidden, and
activate it later when you have real content (research summaries, tutorials,
conference trip reports)
- **Option B**: Start posting now — even 1-2 research summary posts can drive
traffic and demonstrate communication skills

---

## Priority 5: Polish & Refinements

### 5.1 Favicon
Currently using emoji `🧐` as favicon. Consider:
- A custom favicon with your initials "ZW" for a more professional look
- Or keep the emoji if it matches your personal brand

### 5.2 Color Scheme Refinement
Your UW-Madison red (`#c5050c`) as the primary theme color is a strong brand
choice. The teal hover color (`#0099cc`) complements it well. This is good — no
major changes needed. Minor suggestion:
- The blockquote yellow background (`#fffacd`) with red border is visually
strong; consider if you want all blockquotes this prominent, or only specific
callouts

### 5.3 CV Page
- Update the PDF filename reference from `CV_ZhengyangWan_2412.pdf` to a more
current version if available
- Verify the PDF file exists and is up to date

### 5.4 Footer
The fixed footer with "Powered by Jekyll with al-folio theme" is standard.
Consider adding a brief one-liner like "Built with ❤️ at UW-Madison" for
personality, but this is very optional.

---

## Summary: Recommended Implementation Order

| Step | Category | Impact | Effort |
|------|----------|--------|--------|
| 1 | Remove "under development" notice | High | 1 min |
| 2 | Delete template remnants (demo posts, projects, news) | High | 10 min |

| 5 | Enable Open Graph & Schema.org | Medium | 1 min |

| 8 | Move/remove ClustrMaps from homepage | Low | 5 min |
| 9 | Fix VRU project (remove unsplash redirect) | Medium | 5 min |
| 10 | Add Research page (optional, needs content) | High | 30+ min |
| 11 | Expand homepage bio with research vision | Medium | 15 min |

## Critical Files
- `_pages/about.md` — Homepage content and profile settings
- `_layouts/about.liquid` — Homepage layout (ClustrMaps, section order)
- `_config.yml` — Site metadata, OG/Schema, blog name
- `assets/json/resume.json` — CV data (location update)

- `_pages/dropdown.md` — Redundant dropdown menu
- `_news/announcement_2.md` — Template news to delete
- `_posts/*.md` — 30 demo posts to delete
- `_projects/4-9_project.md` — Template projects to delete

# 修改 Venue 标签配色方案

## Context
当前 `_data/venues.yml` 中的 venue 标签颜色饱和度过高（如 `#CC0033`、`#FFFF00`），不符合学术网站简洁优美的风格。需要按三个优先级重新设计低饱和度配色，并统一所有 venue 类别。

## 配色方案

### 三级分类

| 优先级 | 含义 | 颜色 | 色号 | 对应 abbr |
|--------|------|------|------|-----------|
| 🔴 红色 | Q1 / 顶刊 / 顶会 | 低饱和莫兰迪红 | `#C17C74` | SCI Q1, JICV |
| 🟡 黄色 | 会议 | 低饱和暖黄 | `#D4B896` | Conference |
| 🔵 蓝色 | 其余 | 低饱和灰蓝 | `#7C9CB5` | SCI Q2, SCI Q3, SCI Q4, EI, CSCD, Patent, AJP, PhysRev, Vision |
| 🩷 粉色 | arXiv（保持不变） | — | `#FF9999` | arXiv |

> 色号说明：选择了莫兰迪色系（低饱和、高灰度），与白色/深色背景均和谐，文字可读性高。

## 修改文件

### `_data/venues.yml`

\```yaml
"AJP":
  url: https://aapt.scitation.org/journal/ajp
  color: "#7C9CB5"

"PhysRev":
  url: https://journals.aps.org/
  color: "#7C9CB5"

"Vision":
  color: "#7C9CB5"

"SCI Q1":
  color: "#C17C74"

"SCI Q2":
  color: "#7C9CB5"

"SCI Q3":
  color: "#7C9CB5"

"SCI Q4":
  color: "#7C9CB5"

"EI":
  color: "#7C9CB5"

"CSCD":
  color: "#7C9CB5"

"Conference":
  color: "#D4B896"

"Patent":
  color: "#7C9CB5"

"arXiv":
  color: "#FF9999"

"JICV":
  color: "#C17C74"
\```

注意：新增 `"JICV"` 条目（当前论文 `huang2025sky` 使用 `abbr={JICV}` 但 venues.yml 中没有对应条目，所以当前没有颜色 — 加上后即可显示红色）。

## 验证
- 确认 JICV 论文的标签从无颜色变为红色
- 确认 SCI Q3 从高饱和黄色变为灰蓝色
- 确认 CSCD 从紫色变为灰蓝色
- 确认 arXiv 保持不变
- 暗色模式下颜色仍然可读

# 基于优先级的 Venue 标签配色方案

## Context
当前 venue 标签颜色由 `_data/venues.yml` 按 abbr 名称映射，维护不便且饱和度过高。改为在 `papers.bib` 中为每篇论文设置 `priority` 字段（0-3），由模板根据 priority 值决定颜色。这样新增论文时只需设一个数字即可。

## 四级配色定义

| priority | 含义 | 颜色 | 色号 |
|----------|------|------|------|
| `0` | arXiv 预印本 | 低饱和粉 | `#E8A0BF`（保持偏粉风格，略降饱和度） |
| `1` | 顶刊 / 顶会 | 莫兰迪红 | `#C17C74` |
| `2` | 其他期刊 | 灰蓝 | `#7C9CB5` |
| `3` | 普通会议 | 暖黄 | `#D4B896` |

> 均为莫兰迪/低饱和色系，与白色和深色背景均和谐。

## 修改内容

### 1. `_bibliography/papers.bib` — 为每篇论文添加 `priority` 字段

\```
huang2026drivevlmrl:  priority={0}   (arXiv)
huang2025sky:         priority={1}   (JICV 顶刊)
hechao2024tirewear:   priority={2}   (SCI Q3 其他期刊)
zhengyang2024vtt:     priority={2}   (CSCD 其他期刊)
\```

### 2. `_layouts/bib.liquid` — 修改 abbr 标签渲染逻辑

当前逻辑（约第 5-26 行）：从 `site.data.venues[entry.abbr].color` 读取颜色。

修改为：优先使用 `entry.priority` 字段映射颜色，如果没有 `priority` 字段则回退到 `venues.yml` 查找。

替换 bib.liquid 中 abbr 区域的颜色逻辑：

\```liquid
{%- if entry.abbr -%}
  {% assign badge_color = nil %}
  {% if entry.priority == "0" %}
    {% assign badge_color = "#E8A0BF" %}
  {% elsif entry.priority == "1" %}
    {% assign badge_color = "#C17C74" %}
  {% elsif entry.priority == "2" %}
    {% assign badge_color = "#7C9CB5" %}
  {% elsif entry.priority == "3" %}
    {% assign badge_color = "#D4B896" %}
  {% elsif site.data.venues[entry.abbr].color != blank %}
    {% assign badge_color = site.data.venues[entry.abbr].color %}
  {% endif %}

  <abbr class="badge rounded w-100"
    {% if badge_color %}style="background-color:{{ badge_color }}"{% endif %}>
    {% if site.data.venues[entry.abbr].url %}
      <a href="{{ site.data.venues[entry.abbr].url }}">{{ entry.abbr }}</a>
    {% else %}
      <div>{{ entry.abbr }}</div>
    {% endif %}
  </abbr>
{% endif %}
\```

### 3. `_data/venues.yml` — 简化（可选）

保留 URL 映射（如 JICV 的期刊链接），但颜色定义不再需要（由 priority 决定）。可以清理旧颜色值或保留作为回退。建议保留 venues.yml 中的 url 字段但移除 color 字段，使配色完全由 priority 控制。

## 关键文件
- `_bibliography/papers.bib` — 添加 priority 字段
- `_layouts/bib.liquid`（第 5-26 行）— 修改 abbr 颜色逻辑
- `_data/venues.yml` — 可选清理

## 验证
- DriveVLM-RL (priority=0): 显示粉色 arXiv 标签
- Sky-Drive (priority=1): 显示莫兰迪红 JICV 标签
- Tire Wear (priority=2): 显示灰蓝 SCI Q3 标签
- VTT (priority=2): 显示灰蓝 CSCD 标签
- 未设 priority 的论文（如模板中的 Einstein）回退到 venues.yml 颜色

```

## Approved Plan:
# 基于优先级的 Venue 标签配色方案

## Context
当前 venue 标签颜色由 `_data/venues.yml` 按 abbr 名称映射，维护不便且饱和度过高。改为在 `papers.bib` 中为每篇论文设置 `priority` 字段（0-3），由模板根据 priority 值决定颜色。这样新增论文时只需设一个数字即可。

## 四级配色定义

| priority | 含义 | 颜色 | 色号 |
|----------|------|------|------|
| `0` | arXiv 预印本 | 低饱和粉 | `#E8A0BF`（保持偏粉风格，略降饱和度） |
| `1` | 顶刊 / 顶会 | 莫兰迪红 | `#C17C74` |
| `2` | 其他期刊 | 灰蓝 | `#7C9CB5` |
| `3` | 普通会议 | 暖黄 | `#D4B896` |

> 均为莫兰迪/低饱和色系，与白色和深色背景均和谐。

## 修改内容

### 1. `_bibliography/papers.bib` — 为每篇论文添加 `priority` 字段

```
huang2026drivevlmrl:  priority={0}   (arXiv)
huang2025sky:         priority={1}   (JICV 顶刊)
hechao2024tirewear:   priority={2}   (SCI Q3 其他期刊)
zhengyang2024vtt:     priority={2}   (CSCD 其他期刊)
```

### 2. `_layouts/bib.liquid` — 修改 abbr 标签渲染逻辑

当前逻辑（约第 5-26 行）：从 `site.data.venues[entry.abbr].color` 读取颜色。

修改为：优先使用 `entry.priority` 字段映射颜色，如果没有 `priority` 字段则回退到 `venues.yml` 查找。

替换 bib.liquid 中 abbr 区域的颜色逻辑：

```liquid
{%- if entry.abbr -%}
  {% assign badge_color = nil %}
  {% if entry.priority == "0" %}
    {% assign badge_color = "#E8A0BF" %}
  {% elsif entry.priority == "1" %}
    {% assign badge_color = "#C17C74" %}
  {% elsif entry.priority == "2" %}
    {% assign badge_color = "#7C9CB5" %}
  {% elsif entry.priority == "3" %}
    {% assign badge_color = "#D4B896" %}
  {% elsif site.data.venues[entry.abbr].color != blank %}
    {% assign badge_color = site.data.venues[entry.abbr].color %}
  {% endif %}

  <abbr class="badge rounded w-100"
    {% if badge_color %}style="background-color:{{ badge_color }}"{% endif %}>
    {% if site.data.venues[entry.abbr].url %}
      <a href="{{ site.data.venues[entry.abbr].url }}">{{ entry.abbr }}</a>
    {% else %}
      <div>{{ entry.abbr }}</div>
    {% endif %}
  </abbr>
{% endif %}
```

### 3. `_data/venues.yml` — 简化（可选）

保留 URL 映射（如 JICV 的期刊链接），但颜色定义不再需要（由 priority 决定）。可以清理旧颜色值或保留作为回退。建议保留 venues.yml 中的 url 字段但移除 color 字段，使配色完全由 priority 控制。

## 关键文件
- `_bibliography/papers.bib` — 添加 priority 字段
- `_layouts/bib.liquid`（第 5-26 行）— 修改 abbr 颜色逻辑
- `_data/venues.yml` — 可选清理

## 验证
- DriveVLM-RL (priority=0): 显示粉色 arXiv 标签
- Sky-Drive (priority=1): 显示莫兰迪红 JICV 标签
- Tire Wear (priority=2): 显示灰蓝 SCI Q3 标签
- VTT (priority=2): 显示灰蓝 CSCD 标签
- 未设 priority 的论文（如模板中的 Einstein）回退到 venues.yml 颜色
```

13	  abstract={Ensuring safe decision-making in autonomous vehicles remains a fundamental challenge despite rapid advances in end-to-end learning approaches. Traditional reinforcement learning (RL) methods rely on manually engineered rewards or sparse collision signals, which fail to capture the rich contextual understanding required for safe driving and make unsafe exploration unavoidable in real-world settings. Recent vision-language models (VLMs) offer promising semantic understanding capabilities; however, their high inference latency and susceptibility to hallucination hinder direct application to real-time vehicle control. To address these limitations, this paper proposes DriveVLM-RL, a neuroscience-inspired framework that integrates VLMs into RL through a dual-pathway architecture for safe and deployable autonomous driving. The framework decomposes semantic reward learning into a Static Pathway for continuous spatial safety assessment using CLIP-based contrasting language goals, and a Dynamic Pathway for attention-gated multi-frame semantic risk reasoning using a lightweight detector and a large VLM. A hierarchical reward synthesis mechanism fuses semantic signals with vehicle states, while an asynchronous training pipeline decouples expensive VLM inference from environment interaction. All VLM components are used only during offline training and are removed at deployment, ensuring real-time feasibility. Experiments in the CARLA simulator show significant improvements in collision avoidance, task success, and generalization across diverse traffic scenarios, including strong robustness under settings without explicit collision penalties. These results demonstrate that DriveVLM-RL provides a practical paradigm for integrating foundation models into autonomous driving without compromising real-time feasibility.},

27	  abstract={Recent advances in autonomous system simulation platforms have significantly enhanced the safe and scalable testing of driving policies. However, existing simulators do not yet fully meet the needs of future transportation research, particularly in modeling socially-aware driving agents and enabling effective human-AI collaboration. This paper introduces Sky-Drive, a novel distributed multi-agent simulation platform that addresses these limitations through four key innovations: (a) a distributed architecture for synchronized simulation across multiple terminals; (b) a multi-modal human-in-the-loop framework integrating diverse sensors to collect rich behavioral data; (c) a human-AI collaboration mechanism supporting continuous and adaptive knowledge exchange; and (d) a digital twin (DT) framework for constructing high-fidelity virtual replicas of real-world transportation environments. Sky-Drive supports diverse applications such as autonomous vehicle (AV)-vulnerable road user (VRU) interaction modeling, human-in-the-loop training, socially-aware reinforcement learning, personalized driving policy, and customized scenario generation. Future extensions will incorporate foundation models for context-aware decision support and hardware-in-the-loop (HIL) testing for real-world validation. By bridging scenario generation, data collection, algorithm training, and hardware integration, Sky-Drive has the potential to become a foundational platform for the next generation of socially-aware and human-centered autonomous transportation researc},

44	  abstract={The Virtual Track Train (VTT) represents a novel urban transportation solution that combines elements of both road and rail vehicles. Currently, VTT has gained significant adoption in several cities in China including Yibin, Zhuzhou, Harbin, and Yancheng. Despite its growing usage, the unique structure and complex tire-ground interaction of VTT pose significant challenges in terms of tire wear, which has become a pressing issue. This study develops and validates a multi-body dynamics model of VTT along with a finite element model of its tire. The tire-ground contact behavior and boundary conditions are studied from various perspectives to calculate tire wear. The simulation results demonstrate that the internal pressure of the tires, the load of VTT, the pavement conditions, the inclination angle, and slippage of tires all have critical effects on VTT’s tire wear. These findings suggest that the operating conditions of VTT must be carefully managed to reduce tire wear and ensure safe operation. Furthermore, the research results of tire wear mechanism of VTT offers valuable insights for the optimization and maintenance of VTT.},

To further understand VTT’s operation performance, it is essential to develop a specific driving simulation platform to simulate VTT’s unique hybrid driving mode. The main contribution of this paper are as follows: First, a dynamics model of VTT was established using SIMAPCK. Then, the control module for simulating the hybrid driving mode of VTT was developed based on the model predictive control and Logitech driving simulation hardware. Furthermore, a driving scene visualization interface was established using Unreal Engine and Blender to simulate the real driving environment. These contents constitute the driving simulation platform for VTT. The simulation results show that the developed platform can deliver accurate and comprehensive operation performance of VTT under different driving modes, which has strong engineering significance.},
